NoSqlOnSql是SQL的严格语法超集，它为SQL语言添加了面向NoSQL文档的功能。为此，它使用类似JSON的文档，将它们集成到正常的SQL语句流中。
NoSqlOnSql基于扩展SQL数据库来支持NoSQL特性，而无需任何内核更改。从这个意义上说，它类似于其他计划，如https://www.torodb.com或https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/project/json-server-2/.。
然而，NoSqlOnSql的方法不同，不是在处理流程中挂钩中间件，而是在处理开始之前处理NoSQL语义。NoSqlOnSql被实现为源到源转换器：NoSqlOnSql语句被编译成由数据库正常执行的常规SQL语句。此编译可以随时进行，甚至可以在创建数据库之前开发查询期间进行。
NoSqlOnSql能够在没有任何模式定义的情况下创建、更新和查询文档。文档使用类似于JSON的表示法定义。
供应商之间的SQL实现不兼容。除此之外，符合标准的程度也相当不同。这意味着，不幸的是，NoSqlOnSql的每个实现都必须针对特定的SQL风格。NoSqlOnSql的当前实现面向Transact-SQL或T-SQL。这是Microsoft SQL Server中使用的语言。
任何有效的SQL语句都是有效的NoSqlOnSql语句。SQL和NoSqlOnSql语句可以组合使用。NoSqlOnSql被转换成等价的SQL语句。例如，可以从NoSqlOnSql选择查询创建SQL视图。
实现应该尽可能不具侵入性。NoSqlOnSql创建的永久工件的数量减少到一个实现集合中所有文档的层次结构表。
在处理复杂查询时，NoSqlOnSql可能需要创建临时对象，这些对象将在查询完成后丢弃。为避免名称冲突，所有NoSqlOnSql对象都以NOSQL_前缀开头。
如果需要更改某个值，则会创建该值的新版本。结果是：
默认情况下，只有最新版本的值可用，但可以访问文档的更改历史记录。这使得NoSqlOnSql存储成为实施区块链的完美介质。
在SQL层，实现集合只需要支持INSERT和SELECT。也就是说，表上的任何记录都不会被修改或删除。在考虑容错(即复制)和可伸缩性(即分片)时，这提供了许多优势。
NoSqlOnSql语法类似于SQL，它遵循相同的语句、数据集和数据集操作范例。所有NoSqlOnSql语句都以NoSQL开头。
NoSqlOnSql完全集成在SQL中，两种类型的语句及其结果都可以混合使用。
因为一个例子胜过千言万语，下面的例子是不言而喻的。此时此刻，不要太关注语法，只关注大体感觉以及NoSqlOnSql如何与SQL混合。

使用NoSQL查询和支持的SQL方言类型调用Transpile(“MSSQL”代表MS SQL Server)。假结果表示语法错误：

NoSqlOnSql未准备好投入生产。目前，这只是一个概念证明(PoC)，以说明SQL和基于NoSQL文档的差距不是技术差距，而是概念差距。因此，它完全可以用现有的功能加上一些思考来涵盖。

note_en:
NoSqlOnSql is a strict syntactical superset of SQL, it adds NoSQL document-oriented capabilities to the SQL language. For this, it uses uses JSON-like documents, integrating them into the normal flow of SQL statements.
  NoSqlOnSql is based on augmenting a SQL database to support NoSQL features without any kernel changes. In this sense, it is similar to other initiatives like  https://www.torodb.com or  https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/project/json-server-2/.
 However, the approach of NoSqlOnSql is different, instead of hooking middleware in the processing flow, the NoSQL semantics are handled before the processing starts. NoSqlOnSql is implemented as a source-to-source transpiler: NoSqlOnSql statements are compiled into regular SQL statements which are executed normally by the database. This compilation can take place at any moment, even during development of the queries before the database is created.
 NoSqlOnSql is capable of creating, updating and querying documents without any schema definition. Documents are defined using a notation similar to JSON.
  SQL implementations are incompatible between vendors. In addition to this, the level of conformance to standards is pretty diverse. This means that, unfortunately each implementation of NoSqlOnSql must target a specific SQL flavour. The current implementation of NoSqlOnSql targets Transact-SQL or T-SQL. This is the language used in Microsoft SQL Server.
  Any valid SQL statement is a valid NoSqlOnSql statement. SQL and NoSqlOnSql statements can be combined. NoSqlOnSql are translated to equivalent SQL statements. For example, SQL VIEWS can be created from NoSqlOnSql  SELECT queries.
  Implementation should be as un-intrusive as possible. The number of permanent artefacts created by NoSqlOnSql is reduced to ONE hierarchy TABLE implementing all the documents in a collection.
  When processing complex queries, NoSqlOnSql might need to create temporary objects, which will be discarded after the query is finished. To avoid name clashing, all NoSqlOnSql objects begin with the NOSQL_ prefix.
 If a value needs to be changed, a new version of the value is created. As a result of this:
 By default, only the latest version of a value is available, but it is possible to visit the history of changes of a document. This makes a NoSqlOnSql storage a perfect media to implement a Blockchain.
    At the SQL layer, implementing a collection only requires support for  INSERT and  SELECT. That is no record on the table gets never modified or deleted. This provides a number of advantages when considering fault tolerance (i.e., replication) and scalability (i.e., sharding).
  The NoSqlOnSql syntax resembles that of SQL, it follows the same paradigm of statements, data sets and operations on data sets. All NoSqlOnSql statements begin with nosql.
 NoSqlOnSql is fully integrated in SQL, both types of statements and their results can be intermixed.
 As an example is worth a thousand words, the example below is rather self-explanatory. At this moment, do not pay too much attention to the syntax, just focus on the general feeling and how NoSqlOnSql mixes with SQL.
 nosql  create springfield override nosql  insert { name:  " Homer", nationality: " US", age:  34, color: " green", lastname: " Simpson" , weight:90, likes:[ ' football', ' doughnuts'], children: [ {name:  " Bart",lastname: " Simpson",weight:38,age:10}, {name: " Lisa",lastname: " Simpson",age:8,likes:[ ' music']} ]}  into springfield nosql  insert { name:  " Moe",lastname: " Szyslak",occupation: " bartender"}  into springfield nosql  select ?, $name, $lastname,#age, $children[ 0].name  as kid0  from springfield _  where $Lastname= ' Simpson'
  id | name | lastname | age | kid0 ----------------------------------0 1 | Homer | Simpson | 34 | Bart1 4 | Bart | Simpson | 10 |2 5 | Lisa | Simpson | 8 |
      Call  Transpile with the nosql query and the type of SQL dialect supported (“ mssql” stands for MS SQL Server). A false result means a syntax error:
  Code, comments and host callbacks are generated as a result of transpiling, but we are just interested in Code blocks, which are just regular SQL code. As such, we execute them as usual:
 using ( var database= new SqlConnection(connectionString)){  for ( var i =  0; i  < nosql.Code.Length; i++) {  if (nosql.Code[i].Type == BlockType.Code) {  using (SqlCommand command =  new SqlCommand(nosql.Code[i].Value, database)) {  using ( var rs = command.ExecuteReader()) ...PROCESS AS USUAL !! } } }}
   NoSqlOnSql is not production ready. Currently, it is just a Proof of Concept ( PoC) to showcase that the gap between SQL and NoSQL document based is not a technical gap, but a conceptual one. So it can be covered purely with the existing functionalities, plus some thinking.
 The PoC is valid and solid, however the effort required to transform this into an operational product is not something I am willing to step into on my own. So, if you are interested in expanding this, just drop me a line or write a comment.

note:
自动化到位后，不再需要花费时间在手动任务上；您可以在更少的时间内完成更多工作，并且您的职责会被委派给机器，在您做其他事情的同时，机器会在后台完成这些任务。你可以自由地进行新的努力。正如约翰·前田(John Maeda)所说，节省时间让人感觉很简单。
我想你会同意我的观点，在打印机还不存在的时候，抄写员的工作是传播知识的基础，但今天手工复制文档已经没有意义了。
自动化改变了我们对所做工作的看法，增加了我们的生产能力，往往会让相关的人力贬值。
在技术允许的情况下，我们将基本任务降级到不需要任何人工输入的自动化系统中，而其他任务--不那么重要，但更难自动化--最终把我们的大部分时间都花在了体力劳动上。
不费吹灰之力的自动化过程很容易被低估。只需点击一下，您就可以访问数以百万计的在线出版物、书籍和其他内容。再点击一下，书就会被送到你的Kindle上，在家里打印，或者发货到你家。
然而，重要的是要记住，完成一项任务所涉及的劳动量--或者是否缺乏--并不能决定它的重要性，而完成一项任务所需的时间和精力在很大程度上取决于技能。
即使我们给涉及体力劳动的过程赋予了过高的价值，任务的重要性和必要性也应该被定义为独立于完成它所需的小时数和它的复杂性。
尽管如此，难度和专业知识在很大程度上决定了你将从工作中获得多少报酬，而且，随着越来越多的流程自动化，我们将更难找到报酬丰厚的工作。
这种将进程委托给机器的趋势导致了手工工作的低估，除非人为因素提供了一些不同的东西，使其独一无二。
note_en:
With automations in place, the need to spend time on manual tasks disappears; you can do more in less time and your duties are delegated to the machine, which completes them in the background while you do other things. You're free to move onto new endeavors. As John Maeda says, "Savings in time feel like simplicity."
 I guess you'd agree with me that, while the job of  scribes was fundamental for spreading knowledge back when printers didn't exist, there's no point in copying documents by hand today.
 Automation shifts our perception of what we do and augments our production capacity, often devaluing the human labor involved.
 When the technology allows for it, we relegate essential tasks to automated systems which don't require any human input, while other tasks—less important but harder to automate—end up filling the bulk of our time with manual labor.
 Effortless automated processes are easy to underestimate. One click and you've got access to millions of online publications, books, and other content. One more click and the book is sent to your Kindle, printed at home, or shipped to your house.
  However, it's important to remember that the amount of labor involved to complete a task—or the lack thereof—doesn't determine its importance, and that the time and effort required to perform a task heavily depends on skill.
 Even when we assign excessive value to processes that involve manual labor, the importance and necessity of a task should be defined with independence of the amount of hours required to complete it and its complexity.
 Still, difficulty and expertise highly determine how much you'll get paid for work and, as more and more processes are automated, we'll have a harder time finding jobs that pay well.
 This trend to delegate processes to the machine contributes to the undervaluation of manual work, except when the human factor provides something different that makes it unique.
title: 关于自动化第二部分：如果它可以自动化，它将
title_en: On Automation Part 2: If it can be automated, it will

几乎适用于任何场合的线框集合。您可以非常快速、轻松地在网页上显示信息的未来视觉放置、规划网站内容或估计发展。
显示未来元素和内容的网页(屏幕)的可视图表。主要目标是在开发阶段开始之前规划和显示屏幕的信息性和逻辑性内容。存在表示精度较低的低保真度线框，相比之下，具有较高内容细节的高保真线框。
未来网页或界面的最简单、最快速的视觉表示。远程表示如何以及哪些信息将在屏幕上定位。它可以比作一张纸素描。
为了快速向客户或开发团队展示信息应该如何在未来的网页(屏幕)上显示。这反过来又有助于评估项目的发展，并远程想象网站会是什么样子。
尽可能接近最终设计的网页或应用程序的详细布局(有时可单击)。它还用于在早期阶段测试用户体验场景。
用于创建和测试或多或少复杂的界面或网站的布局，在这些界面或网站中，传达视觉架构和用户体验的准确性和细微差别非常重要，从而降低了项目从原型阶段进入开发阶段时产生误解的风险。
在低保真线框的帮助下，您可以在不需要深入了解Web开发的情况下非常快速地显示未来信息在Web页面上的视觉位置。非常适合标准网站、界面和应用程序的初始设计和内容规划。
创建高保真线框需要大量的时间和专业知识，但它们对于创建复杂的界面是必不可少的，您需要在开始设计之前查看用户交互并测试不同的UX场景。
note_en:
A collection of wireframes for almost any occasion. You can very quickly and easily  show the future visual placement of information on web pages,  plan website content or  estimate the development.
    A visual diagram of a web page (screen) showing the future elements and content. The main goal is to plan and display the informational and logical content of the screen before the development phase begins. There are low fidelity wireframes with low presentational accuracy and, in contrast, high fidelity wireframes with higher content detail.
  The simplest and fastest visual representation of a future web page or interface. Remotely represents how and what information will be located on the screen. It can be compared to a paper sketch.
  In order to quickly show the client or the development team how the information should be presented on the future web page (screen). This, in turn, helps to assess the development of the project and remotely imagine what the website will be like.
  A detailed (sometimes clickable) layout of a web page or application that is as close to the final design as possible. It also serves to test the user experience scenario at an early stage.
  For creating and testing layouts for more or less complex interfaces or websites where it is important to convey the accuracy and nuances of visual architecture and UX, therefore reducing the risks of misunderstanding when the project moves from the prototyping stage to the development stage.
  With the help of  Low fidelity wireframes, you can very quickly and without having in-depth knowledge of web development show the future visual placement of information on web pages. Great for initial design and content planning for standard websites, interfaces, and applications.
 Creating  High fidelity wireframes requires a lot of time and expertise, but they are indispensable for creating complex interfaces where you need to see user interaction and test different UX scenarios before starting to work on the design.
title: 低保真网站线框模板集合
title_en: Collection of low fidelity website wireframe templates

华盛顿，2020年10月7日-世界银行今天表示，随着新冠肺炎疫情的破坏加剧了冲突和气候变化的力量，全球极端贫困预计将在2020年出现20多年来的首次上升，这些因素已经在减缓减贫进程。
据估计，新冠肺炎疫情今年将使另外8,800万至1.15亿人陷入极端贫困，到2021年，总数将上升至多达1.5亿，具体取决于经济萎缩的严重程度。根据两年一度的贫困与共享繁荣报告，极端贫困的定义是每天生活费不到1.9美元，到2020年，极端贫困可能会影响到9.1%到9.4%的世界人口。这将代表着向2017年9.2%的比率倒退。如果这场大流行没有震动全球，贫困率预计将在2020年降至7.9%。
世界银行集团行长戴维·马尔帕斯说：“大流行和全球经济衰退可能导致超过1.4%的世界人口陷入极端贫困。”为了扭转发展进步和减贫的严重挫折，各国将需要为COVID之后的不同经济做好准备，允许资本、劳动力、技能和创新进入新的业务和部门。世界银行集团的支持--涉及国际复兴开发银行、国际开发协会、国际金融公司和多边投资担保机构--将帮助发展中国家恢复增长，并在他们努力实现可持续和包容性复苏的同时，应对新冠肺炎对健康、社会和经济的影响。“。
报告还发现，许多新的贫困人口将生活在贫困率已经很高的国家。一些中等收入国家将看到大量人口滑落到极端贫困线以下。报告估计，总数中约82%将在中等收入国家。
世界银行表示，新冠肺炎疫情与冲突和气候变化的压力交汇在一起，如果不采取迅速、重大和实质性的政策行动，到2030年消除贫困的目标将遥不可及。到2030年，全球贫困率可能不会超过7%左右。
预计越来越多的城市居民将陷入极端贫困，这传统上影响着农村地区的人们。
甚至在新冠肺炎危机之前，进展就已经放缓。2017年新的全球贫困数据显示，2015年至2017年间，有5200万人脱贫。然而，尽管取得了这些进展，但在2015年至2017年期间，降幅放缓至每年不到0.5个百分点。1990至2015年间，全球贫困以每年约1个百分点的速度下降。
除了每天1.9美元的国际贫困线，世界银行衡量的贫困线为3.20美元和5.50美元，反映了中低收入和中上收入国家的国家贫困线。该报告进一步衡量了包括受教育机会和基本基础设施在内的多维范围内的贫困状况。
虽然世界上只有不到十分之一的人口每天的生活费不到1.9美元，但世界上近四分之一的人口生活在3.20美元的线以下，超过40%的世界人口-近33亿人-生活在5.5美元的线以下。
新冠肺炎危机也削弱了共享繁荣--定义为一个国家最贫穷的40%人口的收入增长。由于人均收入增长放缓，预计2019-2021年全球平均共同繁荣将停滞不前，甚至收缩。大流行加剧的经济活动减速可能会对最贫穷的人造成特别严重的打击，这可能会导致未来几年共享繁荣指数更低。
与之前的趋势相比，增长包容性降低的前景是一个明显的逆转。在可获得数据的91个经济体中，2012年至2017年期间，有74个经济体的共享繁荣有所增加，这意味着增长是包容性的，最贫穷40%人口的收入增长。在这些国家中，有53个国家的增长惠及了最贫穷的人，而不是全体人口。2012年至2017年，全球平均共享繁荣(最底层40%的人的收入增长)为2.3%。这表明，如果不采取政策行动，新冠肺炎危机可能会引发收入不平等加剧，弱势群体社会流动性降低，以及对未来冲击的抵御能力降低的周期。
报告呼吁采取集体行动，以确保多年来在减贫方面取得的进展不会被抹去，新冠肺炎造成的对抗贫困的努力也面临着同时对世界穷人造成不成比例影响的威胁，特别是冲突和气候变化。
世界银行集团是发展中国家最大的资金和知识来源之一，目前正在采取广泛、快速的行动，帮助发展中国家加强对大流行的应对。我们正在支持公共卫生干预，努力确保关键物资和设备的流动，并帮助私营部门继续运营和维持就业机会。我们将在15个月内部署高达1600亿美元的财政支持，帮助100多个国家保护穷人和弱势群体，支持企业，促进经济复苏。这包括通过赠款和高度优惠贷款提供500亿美元的国际开发协会新资源。
note_en:
WASHINGTON, Oct. 7, 2020 — Global extreme poverty is expected to rise in 2020 for the first time in over 20 years as the disruption of the COVID-19 pandemic compounds the forces of conflict and climate change, which were already slowing poverty reduction progress, the World Bank said today.
 The COVID-19 pandemic is estimated to push an additional 88 million to 115 million people into extreme poverty this year, with the total rising to as many as 150 million by 2021, depending on the severity of the economic contraction. Extreme poverty, defined as living on less than $1.90 a day, is likely to affect between 9.1% and 9.4% of the world’s population in 2020, according to the biennial  Poverty and Shared Prosperity Report. This would represent a regression to the rate of 9.2% in 2017. Had the pandemic not convulsed the globe, the poverty rate was expected to drop to 7.9% in 2020.
 “The pandemic and global recession may cause over 1.4% of the world’s population to fall into extreme poverty,” said  World Bank Group President David Malpass.  “In order to reverse this serious setback to development progress and poverty reduction, countries will need to prepare for a different economy post-COVID, by allowing capital, labor, skills, and innovation to move into new businesses and sectors. World Bank Group support—across IBRD, IDA, IFC and MIGA—will help developing countries resume growth and respond to the health, social, and economic impacts of COVID-19 as they work toward a sustainable and inclusive recovery.”
 The report also finds that many of the new poor will be in countries that already have high poverty rates. A number of middle-income countries will see significant numbers of people slip below the extreme poverty line. About 82% of the total will be in middle-income countries, the report estimates.
 The convergence of the COVID-19 pandemic with the pressures of conflict and climate change will put the goal of ending poverty by 2030 beyond reach without swift, significant and substantial policy action, the World Bank said. By 2030, the global poverty rate could be about 7%.
 Increasing numbers of urban dwellers are expected to fall into extreme poverty, which has traditionally affected people in rural areas.
 Progress was slowing even before the COVID-19 crisis. New global poverty data for 2017 show that 52 million people rose out of poverty between 2015 and 2017. Yet despite this progress, the rate of reduction slowed to less than half a percentage point per year between 2015 and 2017. Global poverty had dropped at the rate of around 1 percentage point per year between 1990 and 2015.
 In addition to the $1.90-per-day international poverty line, the World Bank measures poverty lines of $3.20 and $5.50, reflecting national poverty lines in lower-middle-income and upper-middle-income countries. The report further measures poverty across a multidimensional spectrum that includes access to education and basic infrastructure.
 While less than a tenth of the world’s population lives on less than $1.90 a day, close to a quarter of the world’s population lives below the $3.20 line and more than 40% of the world’s population – almost 3.3 billion people – live below the $5.50 line.
 The COVID-19 crisis has also diminished shared prosperity – defined as the growth in the income of the poorest 40 percent of a country’s population. Average global shared prosperity is estimated to stagnate or even contract over 2019-2021 due to the reduced growth in average incomes. The deceleration in economic activity intensified by the pandemic is likely to hit the poorest people especially hard, and this could lead to even lower shared prosperity indicators in coming years.
 The prospect of less inclusive growth is a clear reversal from previous trends. Shared prosperity increased in 74 of 91 economies for which data was available in the period 2012-2017, meaning that growth was inclusive and the incomes of the poorest 40 percent of the population grew. In 53 of those countries, growth benefited the poorest more than the entire population. Average global shared prosperity (growth in the incomes of the bottom 40 percent) was 2.3 percent for 2012-2017. This suggests that without policy actions, the COVID-19 crisis may trigger cycles of higher income inequality, lower social mobility among the vulnerable, and lower resilience to future shocks.
 The report calls for collective action to ensure years of progress in poverty reduction are not erased, and that efforts to confront poverty caused by COVID-19 also face threats that disproportionally impact the world’s poor at the same time, particularly conflict and climate change.
 The   World Bank Group , one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries, is taking   broad, fast action  to help developing countries strengthen their pandemic response. We are supporting public health interventions, working to ensure the flow of critical supplies and equipment, and helping the private sector continue to operate and sustain jobs. We will be deploying up to $160 billion in financial support over 15 months to help more than 100 countries protect the poor and vulnerable, support businesses, and bolster economic recovery. This includes $50 billion of new IDA resources through grants and highly concessional loans.
title: 新冠肺炎将在2021年之前增加多达1.5亿极端贫困人口
title_en: Covid-19 to Add as Many as 150M Extreme Poor by 2021

如果你所在的地区AT&amp；T用政府资金来换取宽带的部署，那么你就有可能无法获得这项服务--即使AT&amp；T最初告诉你这项服务是可用的。
自2015年以来，AT&amp；T&39；密西西比州分部已经从联邦通信委员会的连接美国基金(Connect America Fund)获得了超过2.83亿美元的资金，作为交换，AT&amp；T&T；需要将家庭互联网服务扩展到超过13.3万个潜在客户地点。正如我们之前报道的，密西西比州公共服务委员会(PSC)指控AT&amp；T向FCC计划提交虚假的覆盖数据。作为证据，密西西比州表示，其调查发现了具体的、具体的例子，表明美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T Mississippi)报告了地点地址……。在地址实际上没有服务的情况下被送达。&#34；
自那以后，AT&amp；T提供了一个解释，证实它提交了关于某些地址的适用性的虚假数据，但表示它仍将满足为超过13.3万个新客户地点提供服务的总体要求。问题在于美国电话电报公司(AT&&T)如何确定其无线家庭互联网服务是否可以覆盖到个人家庭和企业。AT&amp；T使用传播建模软件来绘制覆盖区域，但该软件并不总是准确的。如果AT&amp；T在这些领域部署光纤到户或光纤到节点，这不是问题，但该公司正在通过无线服务履行其义务。
在某些情况下，客户与AT&amp；T预约在AT&amp；T错误地向FCC&39；的普遍服务计划报告为正在提供服务的地址设置宽带服务。
AT&amp；T在上周致FCC的一封信中写道，需要明确的是，通过一次失败的安装尝试，AT&amp；T了解到，只有在[向FCC计划的管理员]报告了这些地址后，它才能在这些地址提供符合CAF II[Connect America Fund]最低性能要求的服务。这封信发表在《密西西比东北日报》(Daily Journal Of Mississippi)上。
AT&amp；T表示，这些地址只占其向FCC报告的地址的一小部分，运营商正在纠正错误。AT&amp；T还表示，它试图超过扩建要求，以便即使在某些地址无法提供服务的情况下，也能达到总人数：
PSC&39；的信暗示，密西西比州的AT&amp；T正在欺骗委员会和消费者，因为它宣传互联网接入服务是可用的，但一旦技术人员到达并检查可用的信号强度，就无法安装该服务。这种担忧是没有根据的。正如委员会所理解的那样，固定无线服务受到地形的影响。AT&amp；T使用复杂的传播建模软件，该软件考虑了地形和杂乱等因素，以确定可以使用FWI[固定无线互联网]服务的区域。然而，也有一些实例，例如PSC记录的那些实例，AT&amp；T随后了解到信号可能不够强，无法保证满足CAF II性能要求的服务，因为例如，客户在她/他的家和服务的蜂窝塔之间有大量的大树。事实上，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)已经努力超越每一个CAF II建设里程碑，以应对这一不可避免的情况。
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)表示，它还识别了更多其无线网络能够服务的地址，即使在丢弃了错误报告的地址后，这也应该会增加它的地址数量。
2015年8月，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)接受了FCC‘s Connect America Fund的4.28亿美元年度付款，该基金有义务以至少10 Mbps的速度向18个州超过110万个农村服务区的家庭和企业提供宽带下载和1 Mbps的上传。这包括密西西比州，AT&amp；T在那里每年获得4980万美元的收入。AT&amp；T&39；在每个州提供指定数量的地址服务的最后期限是2020年底。
由于AT&amp；T承认提交了一些地址的虚假数据，该运营商表示，它将在今年年底前将这些地点从[FCC计划的门户网站]中删除。AT&amp；T表示，目前还不能这样做，因为该门户网站之前没有向运营商提供自动更新或删除地点的手段，目前仍在更新新功能。为了做好准备，AT&amp；T Mississippi在[门户]中维护了一个需要更新的位置列表，包括永久删除一些位置，以及删除其他位置，然后使用更精确的地理编码将它们重新添加回来。
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)表示，它将添加之前认为无法提供服务的地址。AT&amp；T表示，它最近聘请了一家卓越的公司，对AT&amp；T&T接受CAF II支持的所有18个州的整个CAF II地点清单进行地理编码审查。这个地理编码项目已经确定了大量可宽带服务的地点，此前人们认为这些地点不在AT&amp；T&T符合CAF II条件的人口普查区块内，AT&amp；T Mississippi已经确定可以在这些地点提供FWI服务。AT&amp；T将在更新其[门户]条目时添加这些位置。&#34；
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)表示，其修订后的地理编码标准将显示，一些地点可能距离AT&amp；之前报告的地点超过1,000英尺。对于某些地点，纬度和经度的差异可能意味着我们不能再向该地址提供FWI服务。
但最终，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)表示，预计到今年年底，它在包括密西西比州在内的所有18个CAF II州的建筑需求都将超过其100%的要求。
这并不是美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)今年第一次承认FCC提交的文件有误。今年4月，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)向联邦通信委员会(FCC)披露，它谎报在包括密西西比州在内的20个州的部分地区的近3600个人口普查区提供家庭互联网服务。AT&amp；T将这个问题归咎于第三方的地理编码软件。
密西西比州的PSC委员希望联邦政府审查AT&amp；的指控，因为他们在9月29日要求FCC完成对AT&amp；提交虚假数据的模式的全面合规审计。FCC尚未表示是否会这样做。
PSC局长布兰登·普雷斯利(Brandon Presley)对美国电话电报公司(AT&&T；)的最新回应并不信服。普雷斯利在接受“每日日报”采访时表示，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)正试图淡化这一问题。
普雷斯利说，AT&amp；T；向FCC项目的虚假报告表明了一个更大的问题。这个问题有多严重，我们现在还不清楚。他们试图最小化这一点，但这对我来说是令人担忧的。这就是2.83亿美元的责任，而这个责任就是把它做好。
title: AT&T很难弄清楚它在哪里提供政府资助的互联网
title_en: AT&T has trouble figuring out where it offers government-funded Internet

Numan推出的目的是推广勃起功能障碍的可获得医疗补救措施，但此后已扩展到其他领域，围绕订阅药品建立品牌。
它现在已经完成了由Novator牵头的1000万GB的首轮融资，以及Anhemis指数、Vostok New Ventures和Colle Capital。我们正在建立一种新型的医疗保健公司，为男性提供简单易用的健康和福祉问题解决方案…。COVID危机正在激励男性更好地照顾自己，“Numan的创始人兼首席执行官索克拉提斯·帕帕弗劳拉托斯在一份声明中说。
作为资金的一部分，Novator的Birgir Már Ragnarsson加入了努曼的董事会。“到目前为止，努曼资本效率高的执行给我们留下了深刻印象，我们对公司未来的发展方向感到兴奋。健康是在全球范围内仍可解决的最大的经济转型机会之一，我们很高兴能成为努曼旅程的一部分。“*努曼打算利用这些资金继续投资于技术，扩大业务，并壮大团队，NHSX前首席执行官萨姆·沙阿(Sam Shah)最近加入，担任首席医疗战略官。
大多数研究表明，大多数男性在出现问题时不会去看医生，但他们比女性更有可能患癌症、心脏病和超重，也更容易吸烟、酗酒和滥用药物。这就是为什么像努曼这样的初创公司开始显示出吸引力，因为男性可以采取更多的预防措施。
note_en:
Numan  launched aiming to promote accessible medical remedies for Erectile Dysfunction but has since expanded into other areas to building a brand around pharmaceuticals on subscription.
 It has now closed a £10M Series A funding round led by  Novator,   along with Anthemis Exponential, Vostok New Ventures, and Colle Capital.   “We are building a new kind of healthcare company that gives men simple and accessible solutions for their health and wellbeing problems… The COVID crisis is motivating men to take better care of themselves,” said Sokratis Papafloratos, Numan’s Founder and CEO in a statement.
   As part of the funding, Birgir Már Ragnarsson of Novator joins Numan’s board. “We were impressed with Numan’s capital-efficient execution so far and are excited about the future direction of the company. Health is one of the largest economic transformation opportunities still addressable at a global scale, and we’re delighted to be part of Numan’s journey.”    Numan intends to use the funds to continue investing in technology, expand operations, and grow the team, with Sam Shah, ex CEO of NHSX recently joining as Chief Medical Strategy Officer.
 Most research shows that most men don’t go to the doctor when something is wrong, and yet they are more likely than women to get cancer, heart disease and become overweight, and are more prone to smoking, drinking, and abusing drugs. Hence why startups like Numan are starting to show traction, as men can take more preventative measures.
title: 男性健康订阅初创公司Numan完成1000万GB首轮融资
title_en: Men’s health subscription startup Numan closes £10M Series A funding round

顾名思义，Spendesk专注于所有与支出管理相关的事情。该公司为员工发行虚拟和实体卡，允许您设置审批工作流并管理费用报销。它还可以将您的所有发票和收据集中在平台上。
通过将所有内容集中在同一平台上，它可以让您实时控制支出，并节省会计任务的时间。如果您在Spendesk上合并事务处理和收款，则对帐更容易。客户端还可以将数据导出到Xero、DATEV、NetSuite或Sage。
如果花费很大，你可以向你的经理提出申请。如果他们批准了您的请求，您将收到一张一次性虚拟卡来支付这笔费用。
同样，如果你的公司给你一张实体借记卡，你就会得到一个预先定义的预算。你的经理可以为你的信用卡充值大额费用，阻止自动取款机取款，阻止周末交易等等。员工可以通过移动应用程序查看他们的付款，查看他们的卡余额，并添加收据。
Spendesk是一种软件即服务产品，每月收取订阅费。虽然交易可能因经济危机而放缓，但该公司表示，其订阅收入同比翻了一番。在短短一年的时间里，公司的员工从100人增加到200人。
它仍然专注于欧洲各地的中小型公司。有4万人通过他们的公司使用Spendesk。客户包括Algolia、Curve、Doctolib、Raisin和Wefox。该公司已聘请约瑟夫·史密斯(Joseph Smith)担任首席营收官，上图左为与公司首席执行官鲁道夫·阿丹特(Rodolphe Ardant)(右图)。
note_en:
Spendesk,   as the name suggests, focuses on all things related to spend management. The company issues virtual and physical cards for employees, lets you set up an approval workflow and manages expense reimbursements. It can also centralize all your invoices and receipts on the platform.
 By centralizing everything on the same platform, it lets you control your spending in real time and save time on accounting tasks. Reconciliation is easier if you combine transactions and receipts on Spendesk. Clients can also export data to Xero, Datev, Netsuite or Sage.
   For big expenses, you can send a request to your manager. If they approve your request, you receive a single-use virtual card for that expense.
 Similarly, if your company gives you a physical debit card, you get a pre-defined budget. Your manager can top up your card for big expenses, block ATM withdrawals, block weekend transactions and more. Employees can check their payments from the mobile app, see their card balance and add receipts.
 Spendesk is a software-as-a-service product with a monthly subscription fee. While transactions have probably slowed down due to the economic crisis, the company says that its subscription revenue has doubled year-over-year. In just a year, the company grew from 100 to 200 people.
 It remains focused on small and medium companies across Europe. There are 40,000 people using Spendesk through their companies. Clients include Algolia, Curve, Doctolib, Raisin and Wefox. The company has hired Joseph Smith as Chief Revenue Officer, pictured left above with the company’s CEO Rodolphe Ardant (pictured right).
title: Spendesk为其公司卡和费用服务再筹集1800万美元
title_en: Spendesk raises another $18 million for its corporate card and expense service

Facebook周二表示，它正在推出一项新的全球政策，禁止不鼓励人们接种疫苗的广告。
该公司之前有一项针对疫苗骗局的政策，这些骗局被全球卫生组织公开认定。
Facebook仍将允许支持或反对围绕疫苗(包括新冠肺炎疫苗)立法的政府政策的广告。
Facebook周二表示，它正在推出一项新的全球政策，禁止不鼓励人们接种疫苗的广告。该公司之前有一项针对疫苗骗局的政策，这些骗局被全球卫生组织公开认定。
周二，该公司卫生主管金康星和产品管理总监罗布·利森在一篇博文中说，现在，如果一则广告明确地劝阻人们接种疫苗，我们将拒绝它，该公司的卫生主管金康星和产品管理总监罗布·利森(Rob Leathern)周二在一篇博客文章中表示，如果广告明确地劝阻某人接种疫苗，我们将拒绝它。
这项新禁令出台之际，该公司宣布了一系列政策变化，以消除其社交网络中之前一直犹豫不决是否要删除的问题内容。这包括本周早些时候宣布的对大屠杀否认主义的禁令，上周禁止支持QAnon阴谋论的页面和群组，11月3日美国大选后暂时禁止政治广告，上个月禁止任何试图使美国大选结果合法化的广告，以及上个月决定阻止其社交网络上专注于向用户提供健康建议的群组的传播。
脸书仍将允许投放反对政府疫苗政策的广告，包括新冠肺炎疫苗。
例如，Facebook表示，它将允许像弗吉尼亚州一名州代表候选人在8月份推出的那样的广告，其中包括停止强制接种冠状病毒疫苗的文字！所有的药物都有风险，我们认为，如果不知道是否有长期的副作用，单独讨论在疫苗发布前强制接种是不成熟的，也是危险的。
然而，明确劝阻疫苗的广告-包括宣传疫苗无效或不安全等-将被禁止。
CNBC的一位发言人写道，如果一则支持/反对立法或政府政策的广告明确表示不鼓励接种疫苗，它将被拒绝。这包括将疫苗描绘为无用、无效、不安全或不健康，将疫苗所针对的疾病描述为无害，或将疫苗中的成分描述为有害或致命。
这篇博客文章还概述了该平台的计划，即使用其预防健康工具，指导人们了解流感疫苗的一般信息以及如何接种流感疫苗。
它还表示，它正在与世界卫生组织和联合国儿童基金会合作，开展公共卫生宣传活动，以提高免疫接种率。
我认为很多疫苗犹豫不决的研究人员都知道Facebook有促进疫苗犹豫的潜力，华盛顿大学知情公众中心的疫苗研究员科琳娜·科尔泰(Kolina Koltai)说。
这是朝着正确方向迈出的一步，但要纠正已经造成的损害，还有很多工作要做。此外，科尔泰指出，在群组和页面中仍有许多疫苗犹豫不决的内容。
note_en:
Facebook said Tuesday it is launching a new global policy that bans ads that discourage people from getting vaccines.
 The company previously had a policy against vaccine hoaxes that were publicly identified by global health organizations.
 Facebook will still allow ads that advocate for or against legislation of government policies around vaccines, including the Covid-19 vaccine.
 Facebook said Tuesday it is launching a new global policy that bans ads that discourage people from getting vaccines. The company previously had a policy against vaccine hoaxes that were publicly identified by global health organizations.
 "Now, if an ad explicitly discourages someone from getting a vaccine, we'll reject it," the company's head of health, Kang-Xing Jin, and its director of product management, Rob Leathern, said in a  blog post Tuesday.
  The new ban comes amid a series of policy changes announced by the company to rid its social networks of problematic content it had previously been hesitant to remove. This includes a  ban on Holocaust denialism announced earlier this week, a prohibition on pages and groups espousing the  QAnon conspiracy theory last week, a  temporary ban on political ads following the Nov. 3 U.S. election, a ban last month on any ads that seek to  delegitimize the results of the U.S. election, and a decision last month to stop the spread of groups on its social network that focus on  giving users health advice.
 Facebook will still allow ads that advocate against government policies around vaccines, including the  Covid-19 vaccine.
 For instance, Facebook  said it would allow ads like the ones a state delegate candidate in Virginia launched in August, which included the language "STOP FORCED CORONAVIRUS VACCINATIONS! ... All medications have risks, and we believe discussion alone of mandating a vaccine before it's released, without knowing if there's long term side effects, is both premature and dangerous."
  However, ads that explicitly discourage vaccines -- including potraying them as ineffective or unsafe, among other things -- will be banned.
 "If an ad that advocates for/against legislation or government policies explicitly discourages a vaccine, it will be rejected," a spokesperson wrote CNBC. "That includes portraying vaccines as useless, ineffective, unsafe or unhealthy, describing the diseases vaccines are created for as harmless, or the ingredients in vaccines as harmful or deadly."
  The blog post also outlined the platform's plans to direct people with general information about the flu vaccine and how to get it, using  its "Preventive Health" tool.
 It also said it's working with the World Health Organization and UNICEF "on public health messaging campaigns to increase immunization rates."
  "I think a lot vaccine hesitantcy researchers know the potential that Facebook has to promote vaccine hesitancy," said Kolina Koltai, a vaccine researcher at the Center for an Informed Public at the University of Washington.
 "This is one step in the correct direction but still there is much work to be done to correct the damage that has already been done." Moreover, Koltai pointed out that there's still a lot of vaccine-hesitant content in groups and pages.
title: Facebook宣布了一项全球政策，禁止反疫苗广告，但将允许倡导围绕疫苗政策立法的广告
title_en: Facebook announces a global policy banning anti-vaccination ads but will allow ads that advocate for legislation around vaccination policies

note:
Facebook首席执行长扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)和他的妻子普里西拉·陈(Priscilla Chan)周二宣布，将向地方政府额外捐赠1亿美元，用于支付未来几周的投票站租金、投票工作人员、个人防护设备和其他选举管理费用。
这笔捐款是在之前向州和地方政府捐赠3亿美元帮助资助美国选举之后进行的，此前一个保守的法律团体提起诉讼，试图阻止州和地方政府使用私人资金管理选举，这项费用历来是由政府支付的。在此之前，州和地方政府曾捐赠3亿美元，以帮助资助美国大选。此前，一个保守的法律团体提起诉讼，试图阻止私人资金用于州和地方选举管理，这项费用历来由政府支付。
但是扎克伯格对法律威胁无动于衷，他在Facebook上的一篇帖子中辩称，他为选举管理提供资金的决定没有党派政治动机。这篇帖子预计将于周二上线。
“自从我们最初的捐款以来，已经有多起诉讼试图阻止这些资金的使用，理由是声称接受捐款的组织存在党派议程。这是错误的。“扎克伯格在帖子中写道。这些资金将服务于全国各地的社区-城市、农村和郊区-并由无党派组织分配。
扎克伯格和陈冯富珍为美国选举管理提供资金的努力是在特朗普总统今年春天签署了一项冠状病毒救济法案后宣布的，该法案提供了4亿美元的额外资金用于州选举援助，远远低于投票权领导人和国会民主党人寻求的40亿美元。
这对夫妇最初向非营利组织科技与公民生活中心(CTCL)捐赠了2.5亿美元，该中心设立了一个赠款项目，为地方政府扩大选民渠道的努力提供资金，包括免下车投票、临时人员支持、处理选票和申请的设备以及无党派选民教育。他们向另一个非营利性组织选举创新与研究中心(Center For Election Innovation And Research)额外捐赠了5000万美元，以帮助国务卿进行选民教育。
“在过去的一个月里，我们看到2,100多名选举官员对新冠肺炎响应拨款项目产生了极大的兴趣，他们正在寻求资金，以确保全国各地选民的安全、健康的选举选择，”中央选举委员会执行主任蒂安娜·埃普斯-约翰逊周二在一份声明中表示。我很高兴这些额外的资金将使我们能够继续满足巨大的需求。“。
约翰逊说，这笔资金可供所有负责管理选举的地方选举办公室使用，“每一个经核实为合法的合格选举部门都将被批准拨款。”
保守派法律团体托马斯·莫尔协会(Thomas More Society)已在密歇根州、明尼苏达州、宾夕法尼亚州、威斯康星州、佐治亚州和爱荷华州等八个州提起诉讼，试图阻止拨款获得通过。
托马斯·莫尔协会(Thomas More Society)的阿米斯塔德项目(Amistad Project)主任菲尔·克莱恩(Phill Kline)上周在一份公开声明中表示：“选举管理私有化破坏了我们选举的完整性，因为私人捐赠者可能会决定这些州将在哪里以及如何管理数亿美元。”
路易斯安那州总检察长杰夫·兰德里(注册商标)也反对使用私人资金资助选举。该州的选举官员已经撤回了他们对资金的申请，立法机构中的一名共和党人提出了一项法案，禁止这种做法。
扎克伯格和陈冯富珍在公共选举中的重大投资发生在Facebook面临华盛顿新的监管审查之际，包括美国众议院和联邦贸易委员会(Federal Trade Commission)的反垄断调查。
与此同时，Facebook今年在选举保护措施方面采取了比2016年积极得多的方式，当时该公司因在其平台上举办外国造谣活动而受到巨大批评。
该公司本月宣布，将删除涉及QAnon阴谋论的所有附属群组和页面，其中包括毫无根据的指控，即民主党官员和好莱坞名人在寻求破坏宪法的同时从事不合情理的罪行。
该公司还开始删除特朗普发布的虚假帖子，包括最近的一篇帖子，该帖子错误地称新冠肺炎的致命性低于季节性流感。今年8月，脸书删除了特朗普的一篇帖子，该帖子谎称儿童对新冠肺炎“几乎免疫”。
“废除第230条！”特朗普上周在推特上发帖，显然是指美国法律的一部分，该法律保护社交网络免受内容审查决定的诉讼。
该公司还在其平台上向每一位投票年龄的美国人发送带有投票信息的信息，包括如何登记投票的链接，目标是登记400万选民，并帮助至少同样多的人前往投票站。公司领导人宣布了对选举前最后一周政治广告的新限制，以及在投票结束后通知人们合法计票的制度。
选举政府的捐款直接来自扎克伯格和陈冯富珍，而不是他们单独的慈善努力，陈·扎克伯格倡议(CZI)。近几个月来，那里的员工表示，领导层敦促他们放弃可能给Facebook带来不必要审查的项目，即使这些项目与该慈善机构促进正义和机会的目标是一致的。
科技博客Recode在6月份报道称，CZI放弃了一个选民数据项目的计划。华盛顿邮报(Washington Post)8月份报道称，CZI的黑人员工认为，Facebook希望表现出不偏不倚和欢迎保守派的愿望，正在损害其对黑人社区领袖和事业的支持。
扎克伯格和陈冯富珍在宣布新捐款的同时发表声明说：“公共资金不足和全球大流行给全国各地的选举管理人员带来了前所未有的挑战。我们正在加倍承诺，确保每个合格司法管辖区都有必要的资源，让每一个合格的公民都能安全投票并清点他们的选票。”
在华盛顿邮报和ABC新闻联合进行的最新民调中，民主党提名人乔·拜登领先于特朗普总统，54%的可能选民支持他，而特朗普的支持率为42%。
如何投票：找出你所在州的规则。一些州已经开始寄出邮寄选票；看看如何确保你的选票算数。缺席选票和邮寄选票是指同一事物的两个术语，大多可互换使用。除非以压倒性优势获胜，否则我们可能不会在11月3日的总统选举中有结果。你是不是遇到了投票问题？让我们知道。
选举团地图：谁真正投票，他们投票给谁？探索关键人口群体投票率和投票模式的变化如何影响总统竞选。
战场：这是美国的50个政治州。潜入密歇根州、威斯康星州、北卡罗来纳州、佛罗里达州、宾夕法尼亚州、明尼苏达州、亚利桑那州、佐治亚州、德克萨斯州和俄亥俄州，注册预告片，每周三天在你的收件箱中获得更多州，以及更多来自这条路线的新闻和洞察力。
接下来：特朗普和拜登计划在今年秋天再进行一次辩论；以下是关于2020年总统辩论的一些情况。

note_en:
Facebook chief executive Mark Zuckerberg and his wife, Priscilla Chan, announced Tuesday an additional $100 million in donations to local governments to pay for polling place rentals, poll workers, personal protective equipment and other election administration costs over the coming weeks.
  The donation, which follows a previous gift of $300 million for state and local governments to help fund U.S. elections, comes in the face of a lawsuits from a conservative legal group seeking to block the use of private funds for the state and local administration of elections, an expense that has historically been paid for by governments.
 But Zuckerberg has been unmoved by legal threats, arguing in a Facebook post expected to go live Tuesday that his decision to fund election administration does not have a partisan political motive.
   “Since our initial donation, there have been multiple lawsuits filed in an attempt to block these funds from being used, based on claims that the organizations receiving donations have a partisan agenda. That’s false,” Zuckerberg writes in the post. “These funds will serve communities throughout the country — urban, rural and suburban — and are being allocated by nonpartisan organizations.”
 The Zuckerberg and Chan effort to fund U.S. election administration was announced after President Trump signed a coronavirus relief bill this spring with $400 million in additional funds for state election assistance, far short of the $4 billion  sought by voting rights leaders and congressional Democrats.
 The couple initially gave $250 million to the nonprofit Center for Tech and Civic Life (CTCL), which has set up a grant program that funds local government efforts to expand voter access, including drive-through voting, temporary staffing support, equipment to process ballots and applications and nonpartisan voter educations. They gave an addition $50 million to another nonprofit, The Center for Election Innovation and Research, to help secretaries of state with voter education.
   “We’ve seen massive interest in the covid-19 Response Grant program over the last month from over 2,100 election officials who are seeking funding to ensure safe, healthy election options for voters in every corner of the country,” CTCL executive director Tiana Epps-Johnson said in a statement Tuesday. “I’m thrilled that these additional funds will allow us to continue to meet the tremendous demand.”
 Johnson said the funds are available to all local election offices responsible for administering elections and “every eligible election department that is verified as legitimate will be approved for a grant.”
 A conservative legal group, Thomas More Society, has filed lawsuits in eight states, including the presidential battlegrounds of Michigan, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Wisconsin, Georgia and Iowa, seeking to block the grants from going through.
   “Privatizing the management of elections undermines the integrity of our elections because private donors may dictate where and how hundreds of millions of dollars will be managed in these states,” Phill Kline, director of the Amistad Project of the Thomas More Society, said in a public statement last week.
 Louisiana Attorney General Jeff Landry (R) has also objected to the use of private money to fund elections. Election officials in that state have withdrawn their applications for the funds, and a Republican in the legislature has proposed a bill that would outlaw the practice.
 The significant investment in public elections by Zuckerberg and Chan has occurred as Facebook faces renewed regulatory scrutiny in Washington, including antitrust investigations from both  the U.S. House and the  Federal Trade Commission.
   At the same time, Facebook has taken a far more aggressive approach to election protection measures this year than in 2016, when the company faced enormous criticism for hosting foreign disinformation campaigns on its platform.
 The company  announced this month that it was removing all affiliated groups and pages involving QAnon conspiracy theories, which include baseless allegations that Democratic officials and Hollywood celebrities are engaged in unconscionable crimes while seeking to undermine the Constitution.
 The company has also started to remove posts by Trump that are false, including a recent post that inaccurately said covid-19 is less deadly than the seasonal flu. In August, Facebook removed a Trump post that falsely said children are “almost immune” to covid-19.
    “REPEAL SECTION 230!!!” Trump tweeted last week, an apparent reference to a part of U.S. law that protects social networks from litigation for their decisions regarding content moderation.
 The company has also sent every American of voting age on its platforms messages with voting information, including links on how to register to vote, with the goal of registering 4 million voters and helping at least as many get to the polls. Company leaders have announced  new restrictions on political advertising in the final week before the election, as well as a system for informing people about the legitimate counts of the ballots after the polls close.
  The election administration donations are coming directly from Zuckerberg and Chan, not their separate philanthropic effort, the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative (CZI). In recent months, employees there have said leadership has urged them to back away from projects that could bring unwanted scrutiny to Facebook, even if they were aligned with the philanthropy’s goals to promote justice and opportunity.
  The technology blog Recode  reported in June that CZI scrapped plans for a voter data project. The Washington Post  reported in August that Black employees at CZI believed Facebook’s desire to appear unbiased and welcoming to conservatives was hurting its support for Black community leaders and causes.
 “Inadequate public funds and a global pandemic have led to unprecedented challenges for election administrators throughout the country,” Zuckerberg and Chan said in a statement accompanying the announcement of the new donation, “and we are doubling down on our commitment to ensuring that every qualified jurisdiction has the resources it needs to allow every eligible citizen to vote safely and have their vote counted.”
 In the latest Washington Post-ABC News poll, Democratic nominee Joe Biden leads President Trump, with 54 percent of likely voters favoring him vs. 42 percent for Trump.
 How to vote:  Find out the rules in your state. Some states have already started sending out mail ballots;  see how to make sure yours counts.  Absentee and mail ballots are two terms for the same thing, mostly used interchangeably. Barring a landslide,  we may not have a result in the presidential election on Nov. 3. Are you running into voting problems?  Let us know.
 Electoral college map:  Who actually votes, and who do they vote for? Explore how shifts in turnout and voting patterns for key demographic groups could affect the presidential race.
  Battlegrounds: These are  the 50 political states of America. Dive into  Michigan,  Wisconsin,  North Carolina,  Florida,  Pennsylvania,  Minnesota,  Arizona,  Georgia,  Texas and  Ohio, and  sign up for The Trailer and get more states, plus more news and insight from the trail, in your inbox three days a week.
 Coming up: Trump and Biden are scheduled to debate one more time this fall;  here’s what to know about the 2020 presidential debates.

title: 马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)和普莉希拉·陈(Priscilla Chan)不顾一个保守派法律团体的反对，向选举管理人员捐赠了1亿美元，此前他们捐赠了3亿美元
title_en: Mark Zuckerberg and Priscilla Chan donate $100M to election administrators, despite pushback from a conservative legal group, following a previous gift of $300M

note:
在大约一年的时间里，从0到9万的印象，遵循搜索引擎优化的良好实践是帮助我的博客和读者增长的关键。然而，当我开始的时候，我犯了一些可怕的错误，一些懂SEO的人几乎可以说是自我破坏。
因此，我想用这篇博文来回顾一下在处理SEO和Gatsby时给我和其他许多人带来的无数令人头疼的3个问题，以及我采取的解决这些问题的步骤。我希望这将有助于解决你目前在你的精彩博客或投资组合上可能存在的一些问题，而你甚至都没有意识到它们，启动你的受众增长，并在🚀上被发现。
你可能对SEO的幕后工作知之甚少。至少在我看来，它看起来像是一门晦涩难懂、前后矛盾的伪科学，只有营销人员才能理解(剧透提醒)。但是，在获得@monicalent的精彩课程bloggingfordevs之后，它让我更清楚地了解了与SEO相关的内部工作原理和良好做法。引用她第一份时事通讯中的话。
SEO是一种确保搜索引擎能够理解您的页面是关于什么的一种方式，它包含来自权威来源的高质量的最新信息，并将回答搜索者心中的问题。
有了好的SEO，搜索引擎可以知道你的内容是什么，发现你写的所有博客帖子，如果你幸运的话，还会把你弹到给定关键词的搜索结果首位。此外，在Twitter和Reddit上分享我的最新文章只会导致几天的流量激增，而SEO可以帮助你在网站上获得更稳定的流量，并保持更长的时间。后者是我最长时间缺乏的，尽管我已经正确地设置了我的Gatsby网站和SEO组件(或者至少我是这么认为的)。
Gatsby的文档中有一个写得非常好的章节，介绍如何构建一个SEO组件来帮助您入门。然而，单凭这一点并不足以让我的博客在早期被发现，正如你在下面的图表中看到的那样，它代表了我自从开通这个博客以来每天收到的印象数量：
在第一年的大部分时间里，我的博客每天得到的印象不到50次。今天，在解决了我将要谈到的问题之后，我每天获得超过1000次印象，而且还在增长！当然，搜索引擎优化不是这里唯一的组成部分，我今年也创造了更多的内容，并选择了更好的方式来推广它们，但它仍然是你上面看到的增长的重要驱动力。
你重新阅读这篇文章的博客是与Gatsby一起建立的，并托管在Netlify上。遗憾的是，同时使用这两个工具而不注意URL末尾不一致的尾部斜杠/可能会导致一些不希望看到的结果。
其中一个结果是，当读者导航到我的文章时，我的分析中记录了大量301个重定向。在我的博客上，指向我的博客帖子的链接通常是这样的：/post/Learning-in-public，但是当读者点击它时，Netlify会在它的末尾附加一个斜杠，从而重定向用户。
我的朋友们，这对SEO来说是非常不好的。它影响了我网站上几个不相关的领域，例如：
Opengraph图像或Twitter卡片呈现不一致：读者有时会共享链接，不管是否带有尾随斜杠，这将使一些服务很难获得适当的元数据，从而呈现一个简单的链接，而不是适当的预览卡。
网站地图中的无效URL：我的网站地图是在构建时自动生成的，基于我的网站的URL和页面使用Gatsby插件。由于我的URL末尾没有尾随斜杠，它将生成没有它们的网站地图，一旦上传到Google搜索控制台，就会导致大量关于无效URL的警告，因为Google引用了带有尾随斜杠的URL。
因为谷歌已经用斜杠引用了我的博客帖子，所以我决定使用选项2。
这一变化可能看起来微不足道，但它导致了许多奇怪的问题突然消失。此外，在解决我即将开始谈论😄！的问题之前，解决这个问题对我来说很重要。
如果您关注我已经有一段时间了，您可能已经开始阅读Medium上的我的内容了。大约一年前，当我写下这些话的时候，我创办了blog.maxeherkel.com。本网站上所有可以追溯到2019年8月之前的内容最初都是在Medium上发布的。
最重要的是，当我把文章移植到这个网站时，我没有编辑它们，也没有删除中等的文章。这导致了重复的内容，这意味着我新部署的博客在谷歌排名方面与Medium在相同的关键词、相同的内容上来自同一作者展开竞争。
谢天谢地，有一种方法可以避免这种情况：设置规范的URL。这些URL放在您的博客帖子源代码的&lt；head&>中，并指定此帖子是包含该给定内容的原始帖子。
您需要将它们添加到您帖子的&lt；head&>。例如：&lt；link Rel=&#34；Canonical&#34；href=&#34；https://blog.maximeheckel.com/posts/learning-in-public/&#34；/&gt；
去你过去用过的任何有内容的第三方平台，把这个规范的URL添加到你帖子的设置页面中。我按照Medium上的这个指南更新了我以前的博客帖子。
当然，你不能做的第一步，直到你已经修复了任何潜在的尾随斜杠问题，你可能会有类似我刚才分享的问题。
如果您和我一样是Gatsby用户，那么您可能会尝试使用Gatsby-plugin-canonical-urls，它会在构建时将规范的URL添加到站点的HTML页面。
我尝试过，它始终无法放置正确的URL，特别是因为我的网站作为脱机支持有时会在我的HTML页面的&lt；head/&gt；中写入脱机后备URL。在您自己的SEO组件中以编程方式添加规范链接会更安全。有关示例，请参阅下面的代码片段。
注意尾部斜杠的一致性！不一致会导致排名不佳。
如果您联合您的内容，不要忘记添加规范的URL。直到今年年底，我基本上还在与我自己的媒体帖子竞争，错过了更多的流量和潜在的读者。
不要盲目信任Gatsby插件！尤其是那些在页面头部注入内容的页面。如果误用，它们可能会非常有害，甚至你都不知道。
检查您网站的页面来源！通过开发工具进行检查有时不足以确保元标记被正确注入到您的站点中。
确保您的meta标记不会因为依赖SSR时的客户端效应而被阻止呈现。
有疑问的时候：编写测试！我专门写了一整篇关于CI/CD的博客文章，在那里我展示了伟大的测试和伟大的CI/CD渠道如何帮助我保持内心的平静。
如果你想进一步了解如何建立受众，并了解更多关于创建内容和搜索引擎优化的知识，我强烈建议你关注Twitter上的@monicalent以及她的Devs博客课程。她是一位搜索引擎优化专家，我在一份时事通讯中学到了更多关于高效搜索引擎优化技术的知识！
您有任何问题、意见或只是想私下与我联系吗？别犹豫，在推特上给我拍个DM吧。

From 0 to 90k impressions in about a year, following  Search  Engine  Optimization good practices was key to help to grow my blog and my audience. However, when I started it,  I made terrible mistakes that some SEO literate people could almost qualify as self-sabotage.
 Thus, I want to dedicate this blog post  to look back at 3 issues that caused me, and many others, countless headaches when dealing with SEO and Gatsby and  the steps I took to fix them. I hope that this will help to fix some issues you might currently have on your awesome blog or portfolio without even being aware of them, kick-off your audience growth, and get discovered online 🚀.
 You might know very little about what SEO does behind the scenes. To me, at least, it looked like an obscure, inconsistent, pseudo-science that only marketing people could understand (spoiler alert, it still kind of is).However, after getting  @monicalent's awesome course  bloggingfordevs, it made the inner workings and good practices related to SEO a bit clearer to me. To quote her from her first newsletter
 SEO is a way of making sure that search engines can understand what your page is about, that it contains quality up-to-date information from an authoritative source, and will answer the question that the searcher had in mind.
 With good SEO, search engines can know what your content is about, discover all the blog posts you've written and, if you're lucky, catapult you to the top search result for a given set of keywords. Moreover, where  sharing my newest articles on Twitter and Reddit would just cause a spike in traffic for a few days,  SEO helps you get a more consistent traffic on your website, and for a longer time. The latter is what I was lacking for the longest time, despite having set up my Gatsby website and SEO component properly (or at least I thought so).
 Gatsby's documentation has an incredibly well-written section on  how to build an SEO component to help you get started. However, that alone wasn't enough to make my blog discoverable early on, as you can see in the chart below representing the number of daily impressions I got since starting this blog:
     For most of its first year, my blog was getting less than 50 daily impressions.  Today, after fixing the issues I'm about to talk about,  I get over 1000 daily impressions and it's still growing!Of course, SEO is not the only component here, I also created more content this year and choose a better way to promote them, but it is still a significant driver to the growth you can see above.
 The blog you're reading this article on is built with Gatsby and hosted on Netlify. Sadly, using these two tools together without taking care of inconsistent trailing slash  / at the end of your URLs can result in some undesirable outcomes.
 One of these outcomes was that I was seeing a lot of  301 redirects logged in my analytics as readers were navigating to my articles. On my blog, a link to one of my blog posts would typically look like this:  /posts/learning-in-public but when a reader clicked on it Netlify would append a trailing slash at the end of it thus redirecting the user.
 That, my friends, is extremely bad for SEO. It impacted several unrelated areas of my website, such as:
 Opengraph images or Twitter cards not being rendered consistently: readers would share a link sometimes with or without the trailing slash which would make it hard for some services to get the proper metadata and thus render a simple link instead of a proper preview card.
 Invalid URLs in sitemap: my sitemap is generated automatically at build time with a Gatsby plugin based on the URLs and pages of my website. Since I did not have trailing slashes at the end of my URLs it would generate my sitemap without them which once uploaded to Google Search Console would result in tons of warnings about invalid URLs since Google referenced the ones with the trailing slashes.
 As Google already referenced my blog posts with a trailing slash, I decided to go with option number 2.
 That change might look insignificant, but it resulted in a lot of weird issues suddenly disappearing. Additionally, it was essential for me to fix this before addressing the issue I'm just about to start talking about 😄!
 If you've been following me for a while, you might have started reading my content on  Medium. I started blog.maximeheckel.com just about a year ago as of the time I'm writing these words. All the content on this site that dates back to before August 2019, was originally published on Medium.
 On top of that, I did not edit the articles when migrating them to this website, nor did I delete the Medium ones. This resulted in  duplicated content, which meant that my newly deployed blog was in competition with Medium on the same keyword, the same content, from the same author when it comes to ranking on Google.
 Thankfully there's a way to avoid this:  setting canonical URLs. These URLs are placed in the  <head> of your blog posts source code and designate that this post is the "original" post with that given content.
  You need to add them to the  <head> of your post. For example:  <link rel="canonical" href="https://blog.maximeheckel.com/posts/learning-in-public/" />
 Head over to any third party platform you used in the past that has the content and add this canonical URL into the setting page of your post. I followed  this guide on Medium to update my old blog post.
 Of course, you cannot do the first step until you've fixed any potential trailing slashes issues you may have like the ones I shared just above.
 If like me you're a Gatsby user, you might be tempted to use  gatsby-plugin-canonical-urls which adds canonical URLs to your site's HTML pages at build time.
  I tried it and it would consistently fail to put the proper URL, especially since my website as offline support it would sometimes write the offline fallback url in the  <head/> of my HTML pages.You'll be safer to add your canonical links programmatically in your own SEO component. See the code snippet below for an example.
  Be mindful of the consistency of your trailing slashes! Inconsistencies can lead to poor ranking.
 If you syndicate your content,  do not forget to add canonical URLs. I was basically in competition with my own Medium posts up until late this year and missed on a lot more traffic and potential readers.
 Do not blindly trust gatsby plugins! Especially the ones that inject things in the  <head> of your pages. If misused, they can be pretty harmful without even you knowing.
 Check the page source of your website! Inspecting via the dev tools is sometimes not enough to ensure the meta tags are properly injected in your site.
 Make sure your meta tags can't be blocked from rendering because of a client side side effect when relying on SSR.
 When in doubt:  Write tests! I dedicated  a whole blog post about CI/CD where I showcase how great tests and a great CI/CD pipeline help me keep my peace of mind.
 If you want to go further into how to build an audience, and learn more about creating content and SEO, I would highly encourage you to follow  @monicalent on Twitter as well as her  Blogging For Devs course. She's an SEO expert and I learned more about efficient SEO techniques in a single newsletter than I would have otherwise!
 Do you have any questions, comments or simply wish to contact me privately? Don’t hesitate to shoot me a DM on  Twitter.
title: 我犯的SEO错误以及我是如何修复它们的
title_en: SEO mistakes I've made and how I fixed them

Patit FatFs是用于微型8位微控制器的FatFs模块的子集。它是按照ANSIC写入的，并且与磁盘I/O层完全分离。即使RAM小于扇区大小，它也可以集成到内存有限的微型微控制器中。此外，这里还提供了功能齐全的FAT文件系统模块。
由于Petit FatFS模块与磁盘I/O层完全分离，因此需要以下功能到较低层才能从存储设备读取数据。低级磁盘I/O模块不是Petit FatFS模块的一部分，必须由用户提供。参考资料中还提供了示例驱动程序。
Petit FatFs模块是一个免费软件，面向教育、研究和开发开放。您可以将其用于个人、非营利或商业用途，在您的责任范围内不受任何限制。有关更多信息，请参阅应用笔记。
note_en:
Petit FatFs is a sub-set of FatFs module for tiny 8-bit microcontrollers. It is written in compliance with ANSI C and completely separated from the disk I/O layer. It can be incorporated into the tiny microcontrollers with limited memory even if the RAM size is less than sector size. Also full featured FAT file system module is available  here.
  Since the Petit FatFs module is completely separated from disk I/O layer, it requires following functions to lower layer to read data from storage device. The low level disk I/O module is not a part of Petit FatFs module and it must be provided by user. The sample drivers are also available in the resources.
 The Petit FatFs module is a free software and is opened for education, research and development. You can use, modify and/or redistribute it for personal, non-profit or commercial use without any restriction under your responsibility. For further information, refer to the application note.

An operational amplifier has the internal compensation circuit for stability which limits its working bandwidth. Frequency response of the compensated Op Amp has slope of −6 dB/octave or −20 dB/decade. Unity gain frequency defines the bandwidth where the Op Amp is able to amplify a signal. If we multiply the gain and frequency at any point, the result is the same, allowing us to use this parameter to select the appropriate Op Amp. It is called Gain-Bandwidth Product, GBW or GBP. The limited open-loop gain introduces a closed-loop gain and phase error.   But we want to optimize our circuits, right?       The closed-loop gain of the non-inverting amplifier is:      and for the inverting amplifier:     A (1 − β)G = − ————————— 1 + A β
 where:   A is the open-loop gain; β is the feedback fraction.   Let’s calculate, for example, an inverting amplifier with desired gain of 1 when  A = 100,  β = R1 / (R1+R2) = 0.5:     A (1 − β) 100 × (1 − 0.5) 50G= − ————————— = − ——————————————— = − —— ≈ −0.98 1 + A β 1 + 100 × 0.5 51
 and A=1:     A (1 − β) 1 × (1 − 0.5)G= − ————————— = − ————————————— ≈ −0.333 1 + A β 1 + 1 × 0.5
 But to make a real Op Amp stable, A is frequency dependent and there is a phase shift, how you can see in the picture below.      Gain-Bandwidth Product (GBW) = A × F is a constant, and the greater the GBW is, the faster and expensive the Op Amp is. Of course, A cannot be infinity, so we see a shelf at low frequencies due to a finite gain.    The figure shows the difference between the ideal and compensated Op Amp with GBW = 1 MHz. You can see that the cyan line of the compensated Op Amp is always below the yellow line showing its open-loop gain and there must be some margin.   However, the simulator shows that gain at 1 МГц is not -9.55 dB, but about -7 dB due to a phase shift at the output.   The closed-loop gain error versus gain margin for the non-inverting amplifier looks likes that:       This equation is usually used to compute the GBW of an Op Amp:      where:   Q is the quality factor of the filter; G is the specified gain; F3 is the cutoff frequency at -3 dB; 100 is the gain margin.   Why? A low-pass filter with  Q > 0.707 has a peak and we need to multiply the gain with this peak value to account it. The peak value is:     Qβ = ————————————————————— ≈ Q sqrt(1 − 1 / (4 Q^2))
 The most of used filters are low-pass filters for ADCs and DACs. So even for an LPF with 150 kHz an Op Amp with GBW of 15 MHz is desirable.   Texas Instruments’ engineers shared a method to reduce the requirement in [1][2]. Let’s try to figure out how to use their equations in practice.      Let’s compute a 2-nd order LPF with bandwidth of 150 kHz, gain of 1 and quality factor of 0.707.    The required GBW is:     GBW(Hz) = 100 × Q × G × F3 = 100 × 0.707 × 1 × 150 kHz ≈ 10.5 MHz
 Let’s try to use an Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz.   Add R4 in series with C2:     1 1R4 = ———————————————— = ——————————————————— = 2122 ≈ 2.1k (E96) 2 × π × GBW × C2 2 × π × 1MHz × 75pF
 Change R3:      And finally enter the values into a simulator:    The top circuit and green color on the charts: the ideal amplifier. The middle circuit and cyan color on the charts: the Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz. The bottom circuit and yellow color on the charts: the Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz and with the error compensation.   When R3 is too low, the R3’ value may be negative. In such cases you should recalculate the filter with a greater R3 value.      Let’s compute a 2-nd order LPF with bandwidth of 150 kHz, gain of 1 and quality factor of 0.707.    The required GBW is:     GBW(Hz) = 100 × Q × G × F3 = 100 × 0.707 × 1 × 150kHz ≈ 10.5 MHz
 Let’s try to use an Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz.   Add R5 in series with C1:     R3 + R4 ∞ + 0 1R5 = ————————————————————— = ———————————————————————— = ———————————————————— = 1061 ≈ 1.07k (E96) 2 × π × GBW × C1 × R3 2 × π × 1MHz × 150pF × ∞ 2 × π × 1MHz × 150pF
 Change R2:      And finally enter the values into a simulator:    The top circuit and green color on the charts: the ideal amplifier. The middle circuit and cyan color on the charts: the Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz. The bottom circuit and yellow color on the charts: the Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz and with the error compensation.      Let’s try to apply the same method to the Type II compensation network with Op Amp used in switching-mode power supplies.   Consider a Type II circuit with parameters: the zero at 2 kHz, the pole at 300 kHz, the middle gain is 0 dB.    Conservative calculation of the required GBW:     GBW(Hz) = M × Fpole × Gfp = 100 × 300kHz × 0.707 ≈ 21 MHz
 where:   M=100 is the gain margin of the Op Amp at a frequency in times; Fpole is the pole frequency in Hz; Gfp is the gain at the pole frequency in times.   Christophe Basso in [4] gives another calculation:     GBW(Hz) = M × (20 Fcross) × Gfc = 20 × 20 × 5kHz × 1 ≈ 2 MHz
 where:   M=20 is the gain margin of the Op Amp at a frequency in times; 20 Fcross is the frequency with maximum phase boost in Hz with the factor to account the pole position; Gfc is the gain at the Fcross frequency in times.   The transfer function of the circuit is:     (C1 R1 s + 1)H(s) = − —————————————————————————————————————————————— (s Rfb1 (C1 + C2))(s C1 C2 R1 / (C1 + C2) + 1)
 Add R2 in series with C2. The new transfer function is:     (1 + C1 R1 s) (1 + C2 R2 s)H(s) = − —————————————————————————————————————————————————————— (s Rfb1 (C1 + C2)) (s C2 C1 (R2 + R1) / (C1 + C2) + 1)
 Change the C2 value and find the R2 value:     1C2' = C2 − ———————————————— 2 × π × GBW × R1
   1R2 = ————————————————— 2 × π × GBW × C2'
 Now try to use an Op Amp with  GBW = 1 MHz in this circuit.   Recalculate the circuit values using the equations above and see the result.   Adjust the C2 value:     1C2' = 56pF − —————————————————— = 40pF ≈ 39pF (E24) 2 × π × 1MHz × 10k

 And finally enter the values into a simulator:    The top circuit and green color on the charts: the ideal amplifier. The middle circuit and cyan color on the charts: the Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz. The bottom circuit and yellow color on the charts: the Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz and with the error compensation.      There are 2 variants of the circuit: with an Op Amp and a shunt regulator like TL431. We will use the same parameters: the zero at 2 kHz, the pole at 300 kHz, the middle gain is 0 dB, the current transfer ratio of an optocoupler is 1.    The transfer function of both circuits is (C1=Cz, R1=Rz):     CTR Rp (1 + C1 R1) sH(s) = − —————— ————————————————————————— Rd (C1 Rfb1 s) (1 + Cp Rp s)
 where CTR is the current transfer ratio of the optocoupler.   Add Rc in series with Cp. The new transfer function is:     CTR Rp (1 + C1 R1 s) (1 + Cp CRc s)H(s) = − —————— ———————————————————————————————— Rd (C1 Rfb1 s) (1 + Cp (Rp + Rc) s)
 Change the C2 value and find the Rc value:     1Cp' = Cp − ———————————————— 2 × π × GBW × Rp
   1Rc = ————————————————— 2 × π × GBW × Cp'
 Try to use an Op Amp with  GBW = 1 MHz in this circuit. Recalculate the circuit values using the equations above and see the result.   Adjust the Cp value:     1Cp' = 51pF − —————————————————— = 35.1pF ≈ 36pF (E24) 2 × π × 1MHz × 10k
 The Rc value:     1Rc = ——————————————————— = 4.421k ≈ 4.42k (E96) 2 × π × 1MHz × 36pF

 And finally enter the values into a simulator:    The top circuit and green color on the charts: the ideal amplifier. The middle circuit and yellow color on the charts: the Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz. The bottom circuit and cyan color on the charts: the Op Amp with GBW of 1 MHz and with the error compensation. Do not forget that a real optocoupler has a parasitic capacitance and its value should be taken into account.      The described methods help to reduce the requirement to the Gain-Bandwidth of a used Op Amp and cost of circuits.   For filters, they work well with commonly used quality factors below 1.

我最近开始对两个看似完全不同的东西感兴趣：方案/球拍和符号逻辑，所以我决定把这两个结合到一个小项目中，以获得一些乐趣。这场联姻实际上没有看起来那么奇怪，因为由于Sracket专注于元编程，所以它有非常健壮的词法分析和解析工具。在这篇文章中，我们将回顾一些实现简单解析器的策略，这些策略的概念可以很容易地扩展到解析其他东西，如JSON，甚至您自己的编程语言。
在我们进一步讨论之前，最好先介绍一下我们要解析的内容。命题逻辑是符号逻辑的一个分支，它定义了陈述命题的语法和规则集。有了这一点，我们可以定义如下参数：
约翰·斯图尔特去参观世界公园。玛丽亚·斯图尔特去了世界公园。因此，约翰和玛丽亚将一起去参观公园。\textNormal{约翰去公园。}\newline\textNormal{玛丽亚去公园。}\newline\textNormal{因此，约翰和玛丽亚去公园。}\newline约翰和玛丽亚去公园。玛丽亚·斯图尔特去了世界公园。因此，约翰和玛丽亚将一起去参观公园。问：=玛丽亚去公园P  。  Q  ∴P∧Q P：=\textNormal{约翰去公园}\newline Q：=\textNormal{玛丽亚去公园}\newlineP\；\ldotp\；Q\；\因此P\land Q P：=约翰去公园Q：=玛丽亚去公园P。Q∴P∧Q命题逻辑被限制在属于观察性陈述的句子中，比如约翰去公园。没有变量，也没有像全部或部分这样的量词。
随着命题逻辑的非正式引入，让我们看看我们拥有的符号，这些符号将需要放入我们的解析器中：
→  ，  ⟺  \r arr\；，\if→，⟺。
请注意，P→Q\Text{P}\rarr\Text{Q}P→Q读作&#34；如果是P，则读取为Q\Text{如果P，则为Q}如果是P，则读取为Q&#34；。
好了，理论说得够多了，让我们开始编程吧。我们可以使用的主要库可以在parser-tools包中找到，但是这些工具的文档让我感到困惑，而且设置一个简单的解析器也不是很符合人体工程学。幸运的是，还有另一种构建解析器的方法：吹嘘包。
Brag(更好的球拍AST生成器)是一个包，它允许我们以标准的BNF形式定义语法，然后轻松地对该语法进行lex和解析。只需用Raco pkg install brag安装，我们就可以使用它了。
让我们在我们的目录中创建一个名为gramar.rkt的新文件，并将#lang brag放在顶部。这是我们将用来定义BNF语法的文件。对于命题逻辑，该语法可以很好地表示，如下所示：
#lang brag语句：ATOM|Complex Complex：LPAR语句RPAR|语句连接语句|NOT语句连接语句：AND|OR|IF|IFF。
在BRAG中，任何全部大写的字符串都被解释为要包括在词法分析器中的标记，而那些不是大写的字符串是我们必须在语法的其他地方定义的表达式类型。我们将标记ATOM、NOT、AND、OR、IF、IFF定义为我们前面讨论的符号的类似项。我们还使用标记LPAR和RPAR来表示括号。
在这里，我们将语言的语法依次分解为其组成部分。句子可以是原子的(例如P、P、P或Q、Q)，也可以是复合句。复杂表达式是括号内的任何句子，或者是由连接词连接的两个句子，或者是句子的否定。最后，连接词可以是AND、OR、IF或IFF。
让我们在同一个名为parser.rkt的目录中创建另一个球拍文件。我们还需要导入brag/support和包br-parser-tools/lex，后者是Brag使用的默认球拍解析器工具的分支，语法来自gramar.rkt。要安装它，只需Raco pkg install br-parser-tools即可。您现在应该拥有以下内容：
现在，我们可以定义我们的tokenize函数，该函数将接受一个输入字符串，并输出一个令牌列表。它的形式是这样的：
(DEFINE(TOKENIZE IP)(DEFINE LEXER(LEXER-src-pos；；在此定义令牌规则！[(EOF)(Void)]))(DEFINE(NEXT-TOKEN)(LEXER IP))NEXT-TOKEN)。
我们在这里所做的就是设置导入的函数lexer-src-pos，该函数将获取匹配令牌的规则列表，并返回一个对其进行词法分析的函数。然后，通过调用Next-Token，我们在输入端口上递归运行此lexer函数，直到到达特殊的令牌eof，此时我们返回void。
现在，我们可以为要对其执行lex的令牌添加规则：
(定义(Tokenize IP)(定义词法分析器(lexer-src-pos[(char-range#\P#\Z)(TOKEN&#39；原子词位)]；匹配P和Z之间的字符[&#34；^&#34；(TOKEN&#39；和词位)]；匹配&#34；^&#34；作为逻辑AND令牌[&#34；v&#34；(TOKEN&#39；或词位)]；匹配&#34；V&#34；AS逻辑OR令牌[&#34；~&#34；(TOKEN&#39；NOT LIMEME)]；匹配&#34；~&#34；AS NOT TOKEN[&#34；-&gt；&#34；(TOKEN&#39；如果有词项)]；匹配&#34；-&&>；&#34；好像令牌[&#34；&lt；-&&gt；&#34；(TOKEN&#39；IFF lexeme)]；匹配&#34；&lt；-&gt；&#34；作为IFF标记[&#34；(&#34；(TOKEN&#39；LPAR词位)]；匹配&#34；(&#34；作为左括号(&#34；)&#34；(TOKEN&#39；RPAR词位))；匹配&#34；)&#34；作为右括号[空格(TOKEN&#39；空格词位#：跳过？#t)]；跳过空格[(Eof)(Void)]))；在文件末尾返回void，这将停止解析器(Define(Next-Token)(Lexer IP))；移动lexer以查找下一个令牌Next-Token)。
请注意这些规则是如何括在方括号中的，其中第一个表达式是lex的模式，第二个表达式是该模式返回的内容。您可以返回任何您想要的东西(或者添加副作用)，但是我们将返回内置于BRAG中的令牌结构，因为这将与包的解析功能很好地集成。
令牌结构有两个输入：名称(我们在语法中定义了它！)和词位，它是从我们刚才词法分析的模式返回的字符串。因此，当我们添加[&#34；^&#34；(TOKEN&#39；and lememe)]时，我们是在语法文件中定义的令牌语法规则下对字符串&#34；^&#34；进行词法分析。
还要注意，lex包附带了一些用于匹配令牌的函数，比如我们在这里也使用的char-range和空白。
我们现在有了一个函数，可以对任何输入字符串进行标记化，所以现在让我们解析这些标记，这实际上非常简单，只需：
(DEFINE STX(parse(TOKENIZE(OPEN-INPUT-STRING&#34；P^Q&#34；)；生成语法结构(SYNTAX-&>DATUM STX)；将语法转换为它包含的数据。
解析器完成！正如承诺的那样，语法和解析器文件只有33行。
这正是我们想要的数据结构。整个字符串是一个句子，其中有一个复合句，包含标记P、^和Q。
&#39；(句子(复杂(复杂&#34；(&#34；(句子(复杂(句子&#34；P&#34；))(连词&#34；-&>；&34；)(句子&#34；Q&#34；))&#34；)&#34；))(连词&#34；-&>；&#34；)(句子(复杂&#34；~&#34；(句子&#34；R&#34；)。
我们现在可以分析命题逻辑中的任何符号句子。解析是很好的第一步，但就其本身而言，它做得并不多。使用我们生成的数据结构，我们可以创建可视化逻辑树、用求解器导出定理和论点等的程序。我们在这里使用的相同策略也可以应用于解析JSON或其他结构化语言。
note_en:
I recently became interested in two seemingly disparate things: Scheme/Racket and symbolic logic, so I decided to have some fun by combining those two into a little project. The marriage is actually a little less weird than it seems, since Racket has very robust tools for lexing and parsing due to its focus on meta-programming. In this post, we'll go over some strategies for implementing a simple parser, with concepts that could easily be extended to parse other things like JSON or even your own programming language.
  Before we go any further, it would be good to introduce what we'll be parsing.  Propositional logic is a branch of symbolic logic that defines a grammar and set of rules for stating propositions. With this, we can define an argument such as:
       John goes to the park.  Maria goes to the park.  Therefore, John and Maria go to the park.  \textnormal{John goes to the park.} \newline\textnormal{Maria goes to the park.} \newline\textnormal{Therefore, John and Maria go to the park.} \newline     John goes to the park.     Maria goes to the park.     Therefore, John and Maria go to the park.         P : = John goes to the park  Q : = Maria goes to the park  P    .    Q    ∴ P ∧ Q P := \textnormal{John goes to the park} \newline Q := \textnormal{Maria goes to the park} \newlineP \; \ldotp \; Q \; \therefore P \land Q    P  :   =     John goes to the park    Q  :   =     Maria goes to the park    P  .   Q   ∴    P  ∧    Q Propositional logic is constrained to sentences that are observational statements like "John goes to the park." There are no variables, and no quantifiers like  all or  some.
  With propositional logic informally introduced, let's look at the symbols we have at our disposal, which will need to be worked into our parser:
 →    ,    ⟺    \rarr \;, \iff    →    ,   ⟺
 Note that       P → Q \text{P} \rarr \text{Q}     P  →     Q is read as "      If P, then Q \text{If P, then Q}     If P, then Q".
  Okay, that's enough theory, let's get to coding. The main libraries at our disposal are found in the  parser-tools package, but the documentation for these tools was confusing to me, and setting up a simple parser was not very ergonomic. Fortunately, there is another way of building a parser: the Brag package.
 Brag (Better Racket AST Generator) is a package that allows us to define a grammar in standard BNF form, then easily lex and parse that grammar. Simply install with  raco pkg install brag so we can use it.
  Let's create a new file in our directory called  grammar.rkt and put  #lang brag at the top. This is the file we will use to define our BNF grammar. For propositional logic, that grammar can be represented elegantly, like this:
 #lang bragsentence: ATOMIC | complexcomplex: LPAR sentence RPAR | sentence connective sentence | NOT sentenceconnective: AND | OR | IF | IFF
 In Brag, any string in all caps is interpreted as a token to be included in our lexer, while those not in caps are types of expressions that we must define elsewhere in the grammar. We define the tokens ATOMIC, NOT, AND, OR, IF, IFF as analogues for the symbols we discussed earlier. We also have the tokens LPAR and RPAR to represent parentheses.
 Here, we are successively breaking down the syntax of the language into its constituent parts. A  sentence can be either  ATOMIC (e.g       P P    P or       Q Q    Q) or  complex. A  complex expression is any sentence within parentheses, or two sentences joined by a connective, or a negation of a sentence. Finally, a connective can be AND, OR, IF, or IFF.
  Let's create another racket file in the same directory called  parser.rkt. We also need to import  brag/support, our grammar from  grammar.rkt and the package  br-parser-tools/lex, which is a fork of the default racket parser tools used by brag. To install this, simply  raco pkg install br-parser-tools. You should now have this:
  Now we can now define our tokenize function, which will take in an input string, and output a list of tokens. It will have the form of something like this:
 ( define  ( tokenize ip )  ( define lexer 	 ( lexer-src-pos  ;; define token rules here! 		 [ ( eof )  ( void ) ] ) )  ( define  ( next-token )  ( lexer ip ) ) next-token )
 All we are doing here is setting up the imported function  lexer-src-pos which will take a list of rules for matching tokens and return a function that lexes them. Then, by calling  next-token we run this lexer function recursively on the input port until we reach the special token  eof, at which point we return void.
 We can now add in the rules for the tokens we want to lex for:
 ( define  ( tokenize ip )  ( define lexer 	 ( lexer-src-pos 		 [ ( char-range  #\P  #\Z )  ( token  'ATOMIC lexeme ) ]  ; matches characters between P and Z 		 [ "^"  ( token  'AND lexeme ) ]  ; matches "^" as logical AND token			 [ "v"  ( token  'OR lexeme ) ]  ; matches "v" as logical OR token			 [ "~"  ( token  'NOT lexeme ) ]  ; matches "~" as NOT token			 [ "->"  ( token  'IF lexeme ) ]  ; matches "->" as IF token			 [ "<->"  ( token  'IFF lexeme ) ]  ; matches "<->" as IFF token			 [ "("  ( token  'LPAR lexeme ) ]  ; matches "(" as left parentheses			 ( ")"  ( token  'RPAR lexeme ) )  ; matches ")" as right parentheses 		 [ whitespace  ( token  'WHITESPACE lexeme #:skip?  #t ) ]  ; skips whitespace 		 [ ( eof )  ( void ) ] ) )  ; return void at end of file, this stops the parser  ( define  ( next-token )  ( lexer ip ) )  ; moves lexer to find the next token next-token )
 Notice how each of these rules are enclosed in brackets, where the first expression is the pattern to lex, and the second expression is what to return with that pattern. You can return anything you want (or add side effects) but we will return the  token struct built into Brag, since this will integrate well with the package's parsing capabilities.
 The  token struct takes two inputs: the name (which we defined in our grammar!), and the lexeme, which is the string returned from the pattern we just lexed. So, when we add    [ "^"  ( token  'AND lexeme ) ], we are lexing for the string  "^" under the syntactic rules for the token  AND defined in our grammar file.
 Also note that the lex package comes with some functions useful for matching tokens like  char-range and  whitespace which we use here too.
  We now have a function that can tokenize any input string, so let's parse those tokens now, which is really as simple as:
 ( define stx	 ( parse  ( tokenize  ( open-input-string  "P^Q" ) ) ) )  ; produces syntax struct ( syntax->datum stx )  ; converts syntax to the datum it contains
 Parser complete! And as promised, the grammar and parser files amount to just  33 lines.
   This is exactly the data structure we want. The entire string is a sentence, with a complex sentence within it, containing the tokens P, ^, and Q.
  ' ( sentence  ( complex  ( sentence  ( complex  "("  ( sentence  ( complex  ( sentence  "P" )  ( connective  "->" )  ( sentence  "Q" ) ) )  ")" ) )  ( connective  "<->" )  ( sentence  ( complex  "~"  ( sentence  "R" ) ) ) ) )
   We can now parse any symbolic sentence in propositional logic. Parsing is a great first step, but on it's own, it doesn't do very much. With the data structures we produce though, we could create programs that visualize the logical tree, derive theorems and arguments with a solver, among others. The same strategies we used here could also be applied to parsing JSON or other structured languages.
title: Racket：用33行解析命题逻辑
title_en: Racket: Parsing propositional logic in 33 lines

明尼阿波利斯市议会成员在凸起的讲台上找到了他们的真皮座椅，并向外看着会议厅。他们看到的不是一片空荡荡的椅子，而是一片面孔的海洋：总共大约有80张面孔，有年轻的，也有年老的，有黑人，有拉丁裔，也有亚裔，也有白人。议会会议是公开的，但公众通常不会出席，所以安理会成员内部就是否应该暂停规则，给团体留出时间发表意见进行了辩论-显然，会议厅里挤满了一个单一的、统一的团体-发表自己的意见。议员们商议了20分钟，最后决定人民可以有10个。
Vanessa del Campo Chacón起立发言。Chacón是一名来自墨西哥韦拉克鲁斯的移民，在她的小公寓里经营着一家家庭日托中心，她说西班牙语，一个留着胡子的年轻人跪在她身边翻译。这是罗伯托·德拉里瓦(Roberto De La Riva)，Inquilinxs Unidxs Por Justisa(联合租户争取正义)的联席主任，这是一个租户权利组织，也被称为缩写IX。“我离被驱逐还有几个小时或几天的时间，我认为市政府认为这件事还不够切合实际，不足以介入并承担责任，”Chacón说。“我们想要有尊严的家，”她继续说。“我是在为我的女儿和这里所有的家庭祈求。”就在她说话的时候，另外两个房客走近讲台，站在委员会成员身后，展开了一条巨大的黄色横幅，上面写着：“不要驱逐瓦内萨。”
“我很抱歉，”理事会主席丽莎·本德尔(Lisa Bender)插嘴说。“我们不能让人们回到讲台后面。”本德尔是一名棕色卷发的白人妇女，她关于如何在明尼阿波利斯推广经济适用房的想法引起了全国的关注。她很同情房客，但她还有一个会议要开。本德尔还没来得及说完，房间里就响起了赞美诗。一位戴着贝雷帽的黑人站起来大声喊道：“如果我们得不到它呢？”
在法警护送房客离开讲台后，瓦内萨的邻居克洛伊·杰克逊走近讲台，双手合十，好像在祈祷。杰克逊是一名戴着塑料边框眼镜的黑人妇女，她依靠在机场iStore赚取的每小时15.69美元的工资抚养她十几岁的儿子特雷万(Trayvon)。杰克逊说：“我们不知道我们中的任何一个人还能活多久。”“那么，你们打算怎么做才能挺身而出帮助我们呢？”她看着议会，等待着。“你们今晚可以回家了，睡在舒适的床上，”她说。“我们每晚都要思考这个问题。”
这已经不是杰克逊和IX组织者第一次与市议会对峙了。多年来，杰克逊、查孔和该市科克伦社区五栋建筑的其他居民一直在与他们的房东斯蒂芬·弗伦茨(Stephen Frenz)和他的商业伙伴斯皮罗斯·佐巴拉斯(Spiros Zorbalas)进行旷日持久的斗争。租户们动员起来争取更好的条件，抵制驱逐，并参加了租金罢工。他们联合起来，敦促市议会吊销弗伦茨的租赁执照。它最终做到了，剥夺了他收取租金的能力。但弗伦茨仍然拥有杰克逊和查孔居住的公寓。他想让所有人都出去，这样他就可以翻新并卖给出价最高的人。房客们有另一个想法：他们想让Frenz卖给他们。
今天，在大流行的经济中，数百万租房者面临被驱逐的风险。即使是“关爱法案”(CARE Act)提供的扩大条款--对各州微薄的失业保险每周600美元的补充--也不足以保护许多租房家庭免受无家可归之苦。今年5月，休斯顿批准了1500万美元的租赁援助；不到两个小时就用完了。今年6月，当当地暂停驱逐和其他保护措施到期时，克利夫兰和密尔沃基等城市的驱逐人数激增，远远高于平均水平。接下来的一个月，美国经历了有记录以来最大的经济衰退，失业率达到了自大萧条以来未曾见过的水平。无论如何，国会允许联邦政府暂停驱逐和失业救济金失效。8月份，新奥尔良市长要求捐款，以遏制她所在城市的驱逐潮。9月的第一周，就在疾病控制中心(Centers For Disease Control)发布的联邦禁令生效之前，匹兹堡的驱逐申请数量比正常水平高出13%，沃斯堡高出119%，弗吉尼亚州里士满高出310%。根据家庭脉搏调查(Household Pulse Survey)的最新数据，超过六分之一的失业租户拖欠租金，所有租户中有45%的人认为，他们可能会在未来几个月内被赶出家门。
看着这场迫在眉睫的驱逐危机正在形成，我经常想起那些明尼阿波利斯的租户，我在过去的一年半里跟踪了他们。我去报道了他们-保安、商店店员和夜班管理员-因为我想看看当一群租户组织起来对抗一对拥有数百套公寓的房东时发生了什么，截至2016年，
在这个集体痛苦的时刻，当我们的日子充满死亡和恐惧时，一个新的美国正在孕育和呼唤。但是瞬间是由动作创造的。如果我们想要改变世界，关注一群从根本上改变了自己的人可能是有启发性的。
2017年底，罗伯托·德拉里瓦(Roberto De La Riva)敲响了位于第22大道南的克洛伊·杰克逊(ChloéJackson)的门。杰克逊打开门，叹了口气，问道：“你们这些人为什么不停地敲门？”
杰克逊在芝加哥南区出生长大，在美国购物中心(Mall Of America)在客房部聘用她后，于2013年搬到了明尼阿波利斯。她28岁，有一个8岁的儿子。三年前，在他们的母亲杰克逊的姑姑去世后，她成为了三名青少年的法定监护人。她和她的男朋友把食物放在桌子上--她在麦当劳工作，他是机械师--直到她的三个堂兄弟都走出家门。到那时，她需要休息一下，并认为一个新的城市可能会奏效。
在明尼阿波利斯，她在柯克兰(Corcoran)社区找到了一套月租625美元的一居室公寓，步行即可到达湖街轻轨车站。杰克逊给了儿子卧室，睡在起居室里，旁边有一盏橘红色的盐灯和一张8x10英寸的母亲照片。杰克逊做了一系列的工作，最终在机场iStore降落，当De la Riva敲门时，她正在那里全职工作。作为助理经理，杰克逊每天凌晨2：30起床，为她的儿子准备早餐，喂她的猫凯蒂，然后跳上去机场的轻轨，于凌晨3：40到达。来开店。
杰克逊刚搬进来时，她发现她的房东斯蒂芬·弗伦茨(Stephen Frenz)反应相当积极。但看到她邻居居住的单元的条件，其中许多是非法移民，改变了她的观点。杰克逊经常拿出一两个水桶接住漏水。但是要坐在邻居的桌子旁喝咖啡，她经常要跨过六个水桶。许多部队有蟑螂和老鼠，还有肮脏的地毯。(Frenz告诉我，杰克逊和其他租户的漏水已经修复，租户的不干净导致了虫害的侵扰。)。“我感觉很糟糕，”杰克逊回忆道。“这些人不懂英语，我觉得这个人在占他们的便宜。”
尽管如此，杰克逊并不认为自己是那种抗议型的人。她从未签署过请愿书，也没有参加过任何形式的政治组织。但是德拉里瓦告诉她不要再付房租了。她靠在门框上听着。
IX已经对Frenz提起了一项名为紧急租户补救行动(E.T.R.A.)的诉讼，起诉他要求维修和损害赔偿。这是自2015年德拉里瓦与珍妮弗·阿诺德(Jennifer Arnold)共同创立租户组织以来，租户组织采取的首批重大行动之一。德拉里瓦解释说，在他们提起诉讼后，弗伦茨质疑IX是否按照州法律的要求获得了大楼大多数居民的合作。为了证明这一点，Frenz提交了租约和一份关于IX诉讼中下落不明的单元的噪音投诉记录。IX的法律团队成员参观了大楼，看到门外有一个单位穿着童鞋。但他们注意到弗伦茨的说法有出入。在那个单元里，没有像玩具和书籍这样的居家装饰品。一些虫害控制记录将某些公寓列为空置，但重新开具的发票删除了这一名称。IX的律师传唤公用事业公司，发现他们怀疑空置的公寓单元没有电力账户。这似乎是为了掩盖租户的E.T.R.A.案件而设计的欺诈行为。(Frenz拒绝对这些事件发表评论，这些事件最终导致Frenz目前正在上诉的伪证定罪。)。
IX的律师还注意到，大楼将斯皮罗斯·佐巴拉斯(Spiros Zorbalas)列为抵押贷款的责任方。这让他们停了下来。佐巴拉斯获得了该市最臭名昭著的房东之一的声誉，积累了大量违反住房法的行为。当地一家报纸称他为“南明尼阿波利斯的贫民窟主人”，2011年，明尼阿波利斯市吊销了他的租赁执照。2013年，当Frenz似乎从Zorbalas购买了大约35栋公寓楼时，他向市领导人保证，Zorbalas在他以前的房产中没有财务股份。但现在房贷记录上有佐巴拉斯的名字。租户的律师查阅了大量的公共记录，发现佐巴拉斯拥有的公司实体拥有Frenz管理的Equity Residential Holdings的多数股权。换句话说，为了绕过该市的制裁，佐巴拉斯实际上就像IX的首席律师所说的那样，“把建筑物卖给了自己。”
不难理解其中的原因。佐巴拉斯从Frenz手中购买了相当数量的问题债务，如果没有Frenz，Zorbalas可能不得不清算他的整个明尼阿波利斯投资组合，包括数百个单位。佐巴拉斯告诉我：“我赚的钱很多。”“我在没有做任何翻修的情况下，尽可能快地每月租金从500美元到695美元不等。”当这座城市显然不再批准他们的合作关系时，这两个人陷入了太深的困境，无法离开。最终，他们也认为这座城市越界了。弗伦茨告诉我：“我没有理由向世界宣布我与斯皮罗斯有业务往来。”当我问他是否在法庭上隐瞒了与佐巴拉斯的商业伙伴关系时，他说：“我当然隐瞒了。但这在当时是无关紧要的。“。
弗伦茨与佐巴拉斯有生意往来的消息引发了一连串的事件。房客对这两位房东提起集体诉讼，要求退还房租。然后，在2017年12月，该市吊销了弗伦茨的租赁执照，随之而来的是他收取租金的合法能力-这就是为什么，德拉里瓦解释说，杰克逊和她的邻居不应该向弗伦茨支付任何费用。
杰克逊感谢德拉里瓦，然后关上了门。她回想着搬进公寓后的所见所闻。她想起了特雷冯。当天晚些时候，杰克逊先后与一位邻居和另一位邻居谈论了德拉里瓦所说的话。为了与邻居跨越语言障碍，她使用谷歌翻译或招募了一名双语青少年。
法院很快任命了一名管理员来监督弗伦茨在柯克兰的五栋建筑，也就是后来被称为柯克兰五号的建筑，租户们向管理员支付了正常的租金。这五个人没什么好看的。杰克逊的建筑建于1962年，是一个不起眼的三层砖砌长方形，空调设备向外倾斜。Chacón的大楼隔着两扇门，被深灰色的垂直壁板包裹着，有小的混凝土阳台。窗户被打碎了，一些空置的单元里的电器也被移走了。房客们不会错过弗伦茨贴在每一处房产前面的巨大的、朴实无华的招牌，这些招牌是为他的企业公寓商店做广告的。尽管如此，所有的建筑都在同一个街区，房客们在里面安家落户。当家庭讨论留在原地的方法时，一些人考虑了集体拥有这五家公司的荒谬可能性。没有人知道他们会怎么做，但话又说回来，房客们已经熟悉了这种感觉。他们已经开始按照他们的梦想行动，而不是他们的现实--着眼于目标，想出一路走来的方法。
杰克逊开始对购买房产的想法产生好感。她长期以来一直试图避开这条小路，希望过上平静的生活。但是，正如他们在这场运动中所说，杰克逊一开始犹豫不决，然后一下子变得“政治化”。
一种新的住房运动已经在美国各地兴起。在一个接一个的城市里，租房者已经开始将自己视为一个阶级，有着共同的利益和问题，并联合起来反对驱逐、以利润为中心的开发和房东撤资。当新冠肺炎来到美国时，这个国家已经从严重的房地产危机中摇摇欲坠。租户组织者加倍努力。“由于Covid的爆发，人们更加绝望，”成立于1973年的波士顿城市生活/Vida Urbana社区组织的执行董事丽莎·欧文斯(Lisa Owens)说。“但他们也更有条理。”他们更有组织的原因与大衰退有关。
上世纪末的止赎危机使数百万家庭流离失所，其中包括租房者。例如，在加利福尼亚州，估计2010年所有丧失抵押品赎回权的房产中有38%是租赁房产。在房地产崩盘之前，掠夺性贷款人瞄准了黑人和拉丁裔社区，向居民施压，要求他们在风险更高的条件下进行再融资。到2006年，黑人借款人购买的所有住房抵押贷款中，超过一半(53%)是次级抵押贷款，相比之下，白人借款人购买的抵押贷款中，这一比例为26%。“这就像一场海啸，”安德烈斯·德尔·卡斯蒂略(Andres Del Castillo)说，当我与他交谈时，他是东波士顿城市生活(City Life)的组织者。“经济衰退是我们社区的财富被抽走，投入到这股崩塌和重新安排景观的浪潮中。所有的财富都回流到投资者手中，买下了我们的街区。“。
2010年后，租金继续上涨，部分原因是丧失抵押品赎回权的房主转变为租户进入市场。在全国范围内，要价租金中值在过去20年里翻了一番多-未经通胀调整，从2000年的483美元升至2019年的1002美元-远远超过了租房者的收入。仅在2016年，就有370万起驱逐案件提起诉讼，相当于每100个租户家庭只有8起驱逐案件。这一数字远远超过了290万起取消抵押品赎回权的高峰时期。
“我们正处在一个20世纪20年代的时刻，我们可能会看到全国各地的大规模租户行动，这些行动可能会在未来几年展开，”Urban Habit土地使用和住房项目主任托尼·萨马拉(Tony Samara)说。由于数百万美国人因失业或工作时间减少而无法支付租金，全国和地方租户组织者呼吁取消租金，这让人想起了大萧条(Great Depression)期间席卷城市的租金罢工。“我看到人们开始阐明，在这场危机中，可以看到一个多么不同的世界，”城市权利联盟(Right To The City Alliance)的现场组织者戴文·卡尔德纳斯(Davin Cárdenas)说。该联盟是一个专注于租户权利的全国性组织。“很多人都拒绝常态的想法。“正常”本来就是问题所在。“。
当Covid袭击时，包括全国公寓协会和全国低收入住房联盟在内的许多团体呼吁政府大幅扩大租赁援助。但一些租户组织者开始讨论其他事情的可能性：全国范围内的租金罢工。“你要申请一大笔钱，就为了把钱还给房东？”德拉·里瓦(De La Riva)问道，谈到了针对租房者的刺激方案。“我们现在对社会提出的要求是一种不同的权力关系。”
许多人，无论是自由派还是保守派，都认为租金罢工是不合理的，甚至是危险的。政治盟友警告德拉里瓦和他的联席导演詹妮弗·阿诺德(Jennifer Arnold)不要支持取消租房运动，而科维德是一个令人担忧的问题。同样的事情发生在苏珊娜·布兰克利(Susanna Blankley)身上，她是纽约市律师权利联盟的组织者。“我甚至不能告诉你，不仅左翼人士告诉我们这是不切实际的，”她说，“他们告诉我们这是鲁莽的。鲁莽！但我认为重点是，事实上，一切皆有可能。现在我们有了暂缓驱逐令。除非不是，否则一切都是不可能的。我希望生活在这样一个世界里，我们思考的是我们需要什么，而不仅仅是我们认为我们能得到什么。“。
今年5月，全国各地的租户团体协调了一场大规模的租金罢工。仅纽约市和洛杉矶就有2万租房者承诺预扣房租。今年7月，新奥尔良的租户在该州暂停驱逐的禁令到期后封锁了一座法院的入口，以抗议流离失所。租金罢工和驱逐封锁在整个大流行期间一直在持续-就像近100年前现代世界历史上最严重的经济崩溃期间所做的那样。
在整个20世纪，美国的城市租户组织起来反对骗租和不安全的条件，取得了重大胜利。20世纪初为应对突然的租金上涨以及后来缺乏供暖和热水而组织的租金罢工引起了社会党的注意，社会党忽视了租户作为一股潜在的革命力量。社会党领导的租户联盟开始要求国家支持永久负担得起的住房。1923年，美国第一个公共住房项目在密尔沃基的社会党市长丹尼尔·霍恩(Daniel Hoan)的指导下完工，但到1930年，美国仍然是唯一一个没有联邦政府对住房做出承诺的发达民主国家。
随着住房和劳动力市场在大萧条期间同时陷入困境，公共住房运动加快了步伐。在大萧条的早期，数以千计的租户参加了租金罢工和驱逐封锁。城市租户运动变得更加好战，流离失所的暴力遭到了暴力抵抗。
当法警带着驱逐令赶到时，租户们从临时路障后面投掷石块和瓶子。一些人直接冲向警察，用棍棒袭击他们。1932年，布朗克斯的一家报纸的一篇报道回忆起“警察被拒绝驱逐的房客抓伤、咬伤、踢伤、撕毁制服”的场景。在像纽约这样的城市，这些努力迫使立法者通过租金管制措施，并开始建造社会住房。国会回应。
